metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2008-03 (12/08)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2008-03

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: December 1, 2008

We have updated the Work Requirements chapter to include the new voluntary designation for Employment & Training Programs operated in Juneau and Ketchikan. Further information on the implementation will be provided to Southeast offices.

We made some language changes to the interview section of the Application Process chapter in conjunction with the other program manuals. We aligned policy about Native Restricted Lands with the Adult Public Assistance, Family Medicaid, and Alaska Temporary Assistance programs to the extent possible. We also clarified several policy issues that were requested by field staff.

Highlights of these policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov.

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 Definitions

Adds an introductory paragraph at the beginning of MS 600-2 Adds links to defined terms at MS 603 and the Administrative Procedures Manual

MS 601-3E Conducting Interviews

Revises language that incorrectly implied an applicant had to come into a DPA Office for an interview. Clarifies that the Notice of Missed Interview (F000) is sent by the office and not automatically by EIS . Replaces an interview with another interview in the first bullet to clarify that this interview is not the first interview scheduled. Clarifies the policy that prohibits workers from denying an application for no interview before the 30th day.

MS 601-3F Changes Reported During the Interview

Revises language that incorrectly implied an applicant had to come into a DPA Office for an interview.

MS 602-1A(1) Household Concept

Under (b), removes redundant policy regarding proof of separate household status. This policy is already addressed at (3). Replaces (d) with an Exception to clarify that these elderly households can be considered separate from the other people who live with them even though they do not purchase food and prepare meals separately. Removes sections (e), (f), and (g) regarding residents of institutions. This policy is already addressed at (6).

MS 602-1F Work Requirements

Adds introduction paragraph for the Work Requirements chapter Under (2), expands the description of the Employment & Training Program; removes the reference to the six E&T Program areas; and identifies the mandatory E&T Program areas of Anchorage, Fairbanks, Kenai/Soldotna, and the Matanuska-Susitna Valley. Under (2), adds a section addressing the option for exempt individuals to volunteer to participate in the E&T Program and describing the Voluntary E&T Program in the communities of Juneau and Ketchikan Under (3), revises the paragraph to define ABAWD and reference the waiver due to high unemployment

MS 602-1G Determining Work Requirements and Exemptions

Under (2)(e) Unemployment Insurance Benefits, clarifies policy regarding the exemption for UIB recipients. This change reflects the policy in the federal regulations at 7 CFR 273.7(b)(1)(v). Under (2)(f) Employment, adds a Note to clarify that individuals who are employed at least 30 hours a week and receive excluded earned income, such as MASST and AmeriCorps, are not subject to work requirements. Under (2)(h) Students, removes post-secondary from description of school and training program. Federal regulations at 7 CFR 273.7(b)(1)(viii) state that a student enrolled at least half-time in any school, training program or institution of higher education is exempt from work requirements. Under (2)(h) Students, adds a Note to clarify that adults age 18 or older attending high school at least half-time are exempt from work requirements. Under (4) and (5)(b), removes references to Juneau and Ketchikan as mandatory E&T Program communities Replaces reference to Kenai Peninsula with Kenai/Soldotna Under (5)(b), clarifies that if no private or public transportation is available, individuals residing more than two and one-half miles from the local DPA office are exempt from participating in the E&T Program. Under (6), moves the paragraph describing disqualifications to the end of the section following the list of requirements Under (6), adds a Note to clarify that individuals who are not required to participate in the E&T Program but chose to volunteer to participate are not disqualified if they do not complete the assigned E&T activity.

MS 602-1H Caseworker Responsibilities – Work Requirements

Clarifies that caseworkers are expected to give the E&T 4 form to all adults who are not exempt from work requirements.

MS 602-2B(11) Native Restricted Land

Revises section to align language used in the other program manuals. Clarifies that land classified as Native Restricted Deeds is exempt as a resource because it cannot be transferred or sold without permission from other individuals, the tribe, or the BIA . Adds a note to clarify that the BIA keeps records of Native Restricted Deeds. Clarifies that any house or other structure that is permanently attached to the land is exempt even if it is not used as a home.

MS 602-2B(12) Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( ANCSA ) Assets

Revises section to clarify policy and address the various types of assets, including distributions, stock, partnership interests, land, interest in a settlement trust, and cash distributions Clarifies that any house or other structure that is permanently attached to excluded ANCSA land is also excluded from resources.

MS 602-2B(30) College Savings Plans

Adds 529 to clarify that only College Savings Plans under section 529 of the Internal Revenue Code can be excluded under this policy. A personal savings account earmarked for college cannot be excluded.

MS 602-3A(5) Government Sponsored Training Program Payments

Adds reference to the Compensated Work Therapy payments funded by the Veteran’s Administration; these payments are countable earned income.

MS 602-4D(1) Allowable Medical Expenses

Under (h) transportation costs, adds instructions for workers to use the IRS business mileage rate to calculate transportation costs when the household uses its own vehicle for medical reasons.

MS 603-2B Net Income Determination

Under (6), Apply Dependent Care Deduction, removes maximum $175/$200 language

MS 604-2B Recertification: Application Filing Deadlines

Under (1), adds statement: Register these recertification applications on the Register Recertification (RERE) EIS screen.

MS 604-3H Removing a Member from the Household

Revises second paragraph to clarify that a full redetermination of eligibility is not done. Instead, when a household member leaves, the worker simply recalculates the household’s benefits after removing the household member and his/her income. Clarifies that a change might affect eligibility and the decrease may be a case closure.

MS 605-1D Institutions

Under (1)(d), Group Homes, instructs worker to call the director of the group home to confirm it meets the group home definition. Under (1)(d), Group Homes, adds suggestion to contact the Division of Senior and Disabilities Services to determine if the group home is state-certified.

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2009-01 (02/09)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2009-01

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: February 20, 2009

Self-Employment Annualization income standards are based upon the Federal Poverty Guidelines (FPG) for Alaska as set each year by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services. We have updated the Self-Employment Annualization Standards chart to reflect these changes which take effect March 1, 2009.

The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 (also referred to as the Economic Stimulus bill) signed into law on February 17, 2009, increases Food Stamp benefits by 13.6% effective April 1, 2009. Addendum 4 is updated with the new maximum and minimum benefit amounts.

Verification sections were also updated to add information about the SOLQ interface and remove obsolete information about the Senior Benefits interface.

In response to questions from field staff this manual change includes information on how Refugee Cash Assistance and one-time Refugee Resettlement payments are treated for the Food Stamp Program.

Three policy corrections are also included:

1. Adverse Actions Requiring Only Adequate Notice

We corrected the policy by replacing first of the following month with next scheduled issuance date . This means that if the benefit is already issued (i.e., listed on the Food Stamp Issuance History – FSIH screen), the benefit cannot be retracted and closed the end of the month. Instead, the benefit is issued and the case is closed the end of the following month.

2. Definition of Institutions

We removed Certified Retailer or Wholesaler as a separate eligible institution category. The certified retailer or wholesaler status applies only to drug and alcohol treatment centers.

3. Overpayments that Do Not Require a Claim

We corrected the policy to reflect that client-caused overpayment claims, regardless of the amount, are established. The $125 threshold applies only to agency-caused overpayments on closed cases.

Highlights of these policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4A(3) Information from Data Systems and EIS Interfaces

Adds information about the State Online Query ( SOLQ ) interface and clarifies that caseworkers need to check Social Security interfaces only when it appears the applicant or recipient could be eligible for Social Security benefits. Adds note to clarify that the SOLQ interface may be used only to determine eligibility for the Alaska Temporary Assistance, Food Stamp, Medicaid, and Adult Public Assistance programs. Reformats EIS Interface table to clarify that the SDX, PFD , and DOL Wage Match information is accessed through the Interface Inquiry (ININ) item on the INME. Adds SVES and SOLQ to the EIS Interfaces table. Removes obsolete information about the Senior Benefits Program interface. Since DPA is now administering the program, the interface is no longer available.

MS 602-2A(4) Commingled Resources

Clarifies that the exempt money kept identifiable from countable funds need not be in a bank account to retain its exempt status. For example, cash from an exempt Native corporation dividend that is kept in an envelope can be considered separate and identifiable as long as cash from a countable source is not put in the same envelope. Clarifies that exempt resources kept separate and identifiable retain their exempt status indefinitely.

MS 602-2B(1) Home

Clarifies that the future home site exclusion is limited to one lot.

MS 602-2B(13) Real Property up for Sale

Adds definition of real property. Clarifies that personal property like vehicles and fishing permits cannot be excluded under this provision.

MS 602-2D(2) Non-recurring Lump Sum Payments

Revises paragraph describing a non-recurring lump sum payment as a resource in the month received. This revision now conforms to federal regulations and aligns with other program manuals.

MS 602-3B(7) Other Sources of Countable Unearned Income

Reformats section by removing subsections (b) Verification and (c) Budgeting. Verification and budgeting policies are addressed in other sections of the manual. Adds Refugee Cash Assistance Payments as a new section (q) under Unearned Income. Adds note under this new section to clarify that the one-time Refugee Resettlement payment is considered a non-recurring lump sum.

MS 602-3D(4) Loans

Removes requirement that written documentation from the lender and borrower be provided. Alaska Food Stamp Program rules allow verification by documentary evidence or through a collateral contact and prohibit caseworkers from requiring written verification.

MS 602-3D(13) Non-recurring Lump Sum Payments

Revises paragraph describing a non-recurring lump sum payment as exempt income but a resource in the month received. This revision now conforms to federal regulations and aligns with other program manuals.

MS 604-2E Verification Requirements: Recertification

Under (1), adds new section (h) to describe the verification required when a new member joins the household. Under (2) Information from Data Systems and EIS Interfaces, adds information about the State Online Query ( SOLQ ) interface and clarifies that caseworkers need to check Social Security interfaces only when it appears the applicant or recipient could be eligible for Social Security benefits; adds note to clarify that the SOLQ interface may be used only to determine eligibility for the Alaska Temporary Assistance, Food Stamp, Medicaid, and Adult Public Assistance programs; adds SVES and SOLQ to the EIS Interfaces table and removes obsolete information about the Senior Benefits Program interface. In the Direct Data Systems table under ASVS , clarifies that the ASVS needs to be checked at recertifications for new household members who are not US citizens that join the household after the initial certification.

MS 604-3A(3) Changes That Must be Acted on for Semi-Annual Reporting

Under (b), adds Senior Benefits to the list of other program changes that require the caseworker to take action on the Food Stamp case. Since DPA administers the Senior Benefits Program, any changes in the Senior Benefits payment (approvals, changes, case closures) are required to be applied to the Food Stamp case. Under (c), removes reference to BENDEX alerts. These alerts were discontinued in November 2003. Under (c), removes reference to Senior Benefits Program alerts. There are no longer EIS -generated alerts for the Senior Benefits Program.

MS 604-3F Temporary Ineligibility/Case Suspension

Adds paragraph to note to clarify that when the household is ineligible for the month of application but eligible for the second month, the application month is denied, not suspended. Revises second paragraph in note to be consistently written like the first paragraph.

MS 604-4C Adverse Actions Requiring Only Adequate Notice

Adds clarification that the effective date of the case closure or benefit decrease depends on if the action is taken before or after the benefit issuance date (i.e., run date). Adds note and guidance regarding EIS not issuing benefits when the case has an out-of-state address. Under (5), corrects policy by replacing be leaving the state with not be residing in Alaska and by replacing first of the following month with next scheduled issuance date . Adds new item (6) to provide the caseworker instructions on what to do when receiving returned mail with an out-of-state address.

MS 605-1B(3) Budgeting Countable Educational Income

Under (a), refers the caseworker to MS 602-3B(5) and MS 602-3D(1) to determine if the education income is countable or exempt. Under (d), replaces child care with dependent care and removes the reference to child care limits. Under (e), adds two notes that instruct the caseworker to subtract allowable education costs from countable income even if the expense is paid for by excluded income and clarifies that costs for room and board are not allowed.

MS 605-1D Institutions

Removes certified retailer or wholesaler as a separate category of eligible institution and moves it under Alcohol Abuse and Drug Additional Treatment Centers as a condition of eligible institution status for treatment centers. Adds a statement instructing the worker to contact the EBT Help Desk to find out if a facility is an FNS -authorized retailer. Revises and reformats the paragraph regarding adult and minor residents to clarify that it applies to treatment centers. Reformats the paragraph regarding persons not receiving treatment to clarify that it applies only to treatment centers. Adds note to encourage DPA offices to maintain a list of local facilities that meet the definition of an eligible institution. Reformats the second and third paragraphs under (2)(e) to clarify that they apply to (2)(e). Reformats the second and third paragraphs under (3)(b) to clarify that they apply to (3)(b).

MS 605-2D(7) Self-employment Annualization Standards

Updates the Self-employment Annualization Standards table with new amounts that become effective March 2009.

MS 607-3D Overpayment that do not Require Claims

Under (4), corrects policy to reflect that client-caused overpayment claims, regardless of the amount, are established. The $125 threshold applies only to agency-caused overpayments on closed cases.

Addendum 4 Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updates the maximum and minimum Food Stamp allotments effective April 1, 2009 to reflect the benefit increases authorized by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2009-02

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: September 1, 2009

As a result of the Food and Nutrition Service approving the waiver of face-to-face interviews for Alaska, changes have been made to the interview chapter to remove the requirement to document why the face-to-face interview was waived. This change was announced by broadcast on August 4 and is retroactive to June 1, 2009.

In addition, several changes were made to the semi-annual reporting chapter to clarify what actions must be taken on the Food Stamp case due to reports of change for other programs. In some situations, such as when verification is not provided for another program and those benefits end, the Food Stamp case will remain open.

This manual change also includes the new gross and net income limits, standard deduction for households, excess shelter allowance, and heating and non-heating utility standards that are effective October 1, 2009. Minor clarifications and technical changes are also included.

Highlights of these policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov.

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-3 A When Is An Interview Required?

Removes the requirement to conduct a face-to-face interview.

MS 601-3 D How To Conduct An Interview

Changes title from "When Can The Face-to-Face Interview Be Waived?" Removes reasons a face-to-face interview can be waived. Clarifies interviews can be face-to-face, by phone or by correspondence. Clarifies caseworker must conduct a face-to-face interview if requested by the client.

MS 601-3 E Scheduling Interviews

Changes title from "Conducting Interviews"

MS 604-3 A Semi-Annual Reporting

Adds note about acting on reports of change made by a non-household member on behalf of a household member. Further clarifies when Food Stamp benefits are affected by a report of change received for another program. Adds note explaining that no action is needed when DKC staff report making a change on the income screens. Adds note regarding notice requirements when a household leaves the state or returned mail indicates the household is out of state. Removes example concerning UIB alerts since these are no longer received on Food Stamp only cases. Adds titles to examples in order to make the section more user friendly. Updates "Example: ATAP Eligibility is Redetermined” and "Example: Medicaid Eligibility is Redetermined” to clarify that the Food Stamp case is not closed when the client does not provide verification required for another program. Revises "Example: Fraud Discovers Misinformation Reported at Application” to clarify action can be taken on the Food Stamp case when fraud discovers information that should have been reported at the interview. Adds "Example: Report from Office of Children’s Services” to clarify action to be taken when OCS reports a change in household composition.

MS 605-7 Permanent Fund Dividend Special Processing

Removes specific dates for PFD distribution.

MS 605-7 B Case Processing With PFD Income and Resources

Specifies that item 4, Setting the Food Stamp Certification Period, applies to applicant households. Adds reference to MS 605-7 C for policy on certification periods at recertification during the PFD season.

MS 605-7 C Recertification During PFD Hold Harmless Period

Clarifies the start date of the certification period is the 1st month even when it is a PFD HH month and adds an example.

Addendum 4 Standards and Maximum Allotments

Decreases the standard deduction for households of 5 or less to $241 and increases the standard deduction for households of 6 or more to $256. Updates the gross and net income limits Updates the heating and non-heating utility standards NOTE: These changes are effective October 1, 2009, and will be made via the EIS mass change job scheduled for the end of August.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2010-01

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: January 4, 2010

We are clarifying that verbal verification of self-employment income and expenses is allowable, although written verification is preferred if available. Verbal verification can be received from collateral contacts or the self-employed individual.

Other highlights to policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Replaces the word "attend" with "participate" to reflect that face to face interviews are no longer required.

MS 601-4 A Verification

In the Direct Data Systems table, replaces the Automatic Status Verification System ( ASVS ) with Verification Information System ( VIS ). The VIS is the online electronic verification process used by the SAVE Program.

MS 602-1 A Excluded Household

Clarifies that Alaska courts do not expunge drug felony convictions.

MS 602-1 D Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status, Ineligible Alien

Replaces obsolete INS Form G-641, Application for Verification of Information, with INS Form G-845, Document Verification Request.

Corrects references to the Automated Status Verification System ( ASVS ) with Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements ( SAVE ) Program.

MS 602-2 B Exempt Resources

Clarifies that Aleut reparation payments received from a trust established by the Secretary of the Interior are countable resources if retained.

Adds the date Agent Orange settlement payments ended.

MS 602-3 A Earned Income

Adds a link to 605-2 D , Income from Self-Employment.

MS 602-3 D Exempt Income

Clarifies that Aleut reparation payments received from a trust established by the Secretary of the Interior are countable income.

Adds the date Agent Orange settlement payments ended. Also clarifies VA disability payments for diseases related to Agent Orange exposure are not exempt.

MS 603-3 Determining Monthly Allotment

Replaces the word "attend" with "participate" to reflect that face to face interviews are no longer required.

MS 604-2 Recertification

Replaces the word "attend" with "participate" to reflect that face to face interviews are no longer required.

Removes the requirement for a face-to-face interview.

Corrects references to the Automated Status Verification System ( ASVS ) with Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements ( SAVE ) Program.

MS 605-1 B Students

Updates the mileage rates with new amounts.

MS 605-1 D Institutions

Replaces the word "attend" with "participate" to reflect that face to face interviews are no longer required.

MS 605-2 D Income from Self-Employment

Updates the mileage rates with new amounts.

Clarifies that verbal verification of self-employment income and expenses is allowed.

Addendum 6

Corrects references to the Automated Status Verification System ( ASVS ) with Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements ( SAVE ) Program.

Replaces obsolete INS Form G-641, Application for Verification of Information, with INS Form G-845, Document Verification Request.

Updates table showing amount of earnings needed to earn one quarter of coverage.

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2010-02 (04/10)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2010-02

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: April 1, 2010

We are making several changes to incorporate new policy, clarify existing policy and align policies within our program policy manuals. This includes:

An Eligibility Review Form ( GEN 72 ) may be used as an initial application form if it is received in the month following a case closure. The case closure may be for any reason. Interface verification policies are changed to more clearly describe when an interface must be checked. It is no longer necessary to check all interfaces at each application, recertification, or review. Beginning December 19, 2009, Afghan and Iraqi special immigrants are treated the same as refugees and are not subject to a five-year bar. A non-transferable Limited Entry Fishing Permit is an exempt resource. Funds held in a dedicated account established to hold a minor's retroactive SSI benefits are an exempt resource. Vehicle resource policies have been clarified in the Family Medicaid, Food Stamp, and Temporary Assistance program manuals. Staff can now accept a client's statement for vehicle value, ownership, and use. Additional verification will only be necessary if the information is questionable. Income received from the lease of a Limited Entry Fishing Permit is treated as unearned rental income.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Other highlights to policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-2 A What Is An Acceptable Application Form?

Clarifies that the GEN 72 , Eligibility Review Form, can be accepted as an initial application in the month following case closure regardless of the reason for closure.

MS 601-4 A Verification Requirements for Initial Application

Clarifies which interfaces must be checked at every application and review, and which should be checked if the caseworker receives questionable information.

MS 602-1 A Exceptions to Household Definitions

Clarifies that children who are not under parental control are not required to be added to the Food Stamp case.

MS 602-1 D Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Adds Afghan and Iraqi special immigrants to the list of qualified aliens. Adds Afghan and Iraqi special immigrants to the list of alien eligibility requirements that must be met in addition to being a qualified alien. Adds information about the type of proof that may be used to verify Afghan and Iraqi special immigrant status.

MS 602-2 B Exempt Resources

Clarifies that a Limited Entry Fishing Permit that Commercial Fisheries Entry Commission ( CFEC ) has classified as non-transferable has no value.

Clarifies that funds held in a dedicated account are exempt.

MS 602-2 C Vehicles

Clarifies that staff can now accept a client's statement for vehicle value, ownership, and use. Additional verification will only be necessary if the information is questionable.

MS 602-3 B Unearned Income

Clarifies that rental income received in a lump sum shall be averaged over the months of intended use. Clarifies that income from the lease of a limited entry fishing permit is considered unearned rental income.

MS 602-4 D Allowable Medical Expenses

Clarifies expenses for service animals are not limited to dogs.

MS 602-4 E Shelter Deduction

Clarifies lease agreements and /or contact with the landlord are acceptable forms of verification of shelter and utility expenses. Clarifies that shelter or utility expenses are allowed if in a non-household member’s name as long as the household can verify they are responsible for the expense.

MS 604-2 E Verification Requirements: Recertification

Clarifies which interfaces must be checked at every application and review, and which should be checked if the caseworker receives questionable information.

MS 604-3 A Semi-Annual Reporting

Adds note to clarify that reports of change which result from clients moving from one area of the state to another may result in a change to their benefit.

MS 605-2 C Non-household Members

Clarifies that children are not generally considered visitors in their parent's home.

MS 605-2 D Income From Self-Employment

Clarifies that rental income received in a lump sum shall be averaged over the months of intended use. Clarifies that income from the lease of a limited entry fishing permit is considered unearned rental income.

Addendum 6

Updates link for caseworkers to view documents used to verify alien status. Adds information about Afghan and Iraqi special immigrants.

Addendum 9

Updates list of approved treatment centers.

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2010-03 (10/10)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2010-03

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: October 1, 2010

We are making several changes to incorporate new policy, clarify existing policy, and align policies within our program policy manuals. This includes:

To the extent possible, we are aligning policies in the U.S. United States Citizenship and Alien Status chapter with those in our other program manuals. Any amount of Flexible Health Benefits that the employer does not pay directly to the employee is exempt income. New policy allows a 50% deduction from self employment income for self-employment expenses. The household can choose to use actual expenses if they believe they are higher than 50%.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-1 C SSI Recipients From California

Adds a new section explaining the SSI/SSP program. In California SSI recipients receive additional cash from SSI in place of Food Stamps. They are not eligible for FS in any month they receive SSI/SSP.

MS 601-3 E Scheduling Interviews

Removes the requirement to send a written notice to the applicant informing them of their interview date and time. Includes three options for informing the applicant of their appointment date and time as well as documentation requirements for each option.

MS 602-1 D U.S. Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Aligns policies in the U.S. Citizenship and Alien Status chapter with those in our other program manuals.

MS 602-2 F Conversion or Sale of a Resource

Adds a new section clarifying how to address conversion of resource issues.

MS 602-3 A Earned Income

Clarifies that only the contribution amount that goes directly to the employee (benefit contribution excess) is countable income. Any amount that the employer pays for mandatory health insurance benefits is not counted as income to the employee. Renumbers remaining sub-sections

MS 602-4 D Medical Deductions

Removes mileage rate table and adds a link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates.

MS 605-1 B Students

Removes mileage rate table and adds a link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates.

MS 605-2 D Income from Self-employment

Removes mileage rate table and adds a link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates. Adds new policy that allows a 50% deduction from self-employment income for self-employment expenses. The household can choose to use actual expenses if they believe they are higher than 50%.

Addendum 4 Standards and Maximum Allotments

Increases the standard deduction for households of 5 or less to $243. Decreases the excess shelter deduction to $732. Updates the heating and non-heating utility standards for Northern, South Central and Southeast regions

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2011-01 (01/11)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-01

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: January 3, 2011

We are making several changes to incorporate new policy, clarify existing policy, and align policies within our program policy manuals. These changes include:

New policy surrounding shelter expenses and deductions. Shelter expenses only need to be verified if questionable and a new section clarifying questionable situations has been added. A shelter deduction of $143 is added for a homeless person having some shelter expense.

Revisions to sections referencing the E&T Program as participation is now voluntary, not mandatory. Instructions for caseworkers include how to explain the new policy to clients and how to code EIS .

Examples clarifying use of the Self-Employment Standard Deduction.

Clarification surrounding corrective action and overpayments in expedited service cases.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-3E Scheduling Interviews

Changes wording "from application filing date" to "after application filing date".

MS 601-3F Changes Reported During the Interview

Clarifies the one-day, one-month rule and how it affects the benefit when a new household member is added or moves out before the application is worked.

MS 601-4A Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Adds new policy stating verification of shelter expenses is only required if the expense is questionable. Adds link to new sub section in section 602-4E clarifying questionable situations.

MS 602-1C Identity

Adds SVES citizenship verification to list of acceptable methods to verify identity.

MS 602-1F Work Requirements

Removes all references to mandatory E&T Program since participation is now voluntary state wide.

MS 602-1G Determining Work Requirements

Removes policy regarding exemptions to the E&T Program since participation is now voluntary. Removes policy regarding E&T Program requirements since participation is now voluntary.

MS 602-1H Caseworker Responsibilities

Adds instructions to explain the E&T Program to clients and how to code them as volunteers. Removes the E&T penalty codes as participation is now voluntary. There are no longer penalties for failure to participate.

MS 602-1K Work Requirements Disqualification Procedures

Removes policy that requires the disqualification of a household member for failure to comply with E&T program requirements. Revises the example to a household member failing to attend the E&T assessment as it is no longer required.

MS 602-1M Good Cause Provisions

Removes E&T from the first paragraph. Good cause is not needed for failure to participate in the E&T program because participation is now voluntary.

MS 602-4E Shelter Deduction

Adds policy to explain new Homeless Shelter Deduction. Adds new policy to verify shelter expenses only when questionable. Adds a link to the Department of Labor's Alaska Annual Rental Market Survey that shows fair market rent in different areas of Alaska. Adds examples to clarify when verification of shelter expenses should be sought.

MS 604-2E Verification Requirements Recertification

Adds section with new policy stating verification of shelter expenses is only required if the expense is questionable.

MS 604-3E Verification Requirements: Changes Reported During the Certification Period

Adds new policy stating verification of shelter expenses is only required if the expense is questionable.

MS 605- 2D3 Costs of Doing Business

Adds examples to clarify when the self-employment standard deduction should be used.

MS 605-3B Expedited Service

Clarifies when corrective action should be taken when verification is waived for expedite service applications.

Previous Section Next Section

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-02

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: February 1, 2011

As a result of feedback from the field, we have made a few changes to the Food Stamp manual to further clarify when staff should verify shelter deductions. As always, we appreciate your input and strive to improve our manuals and policies to help those in the field.

We have also updated caseworker responsibilities for the E&T program to remove reference to the E&T 4 form and include new notices, F001 and X081.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1H Caseworker Responsibilities

Removes reference to the E&T 4 form as this form is no longer used. Adds information about the new notices, F001 and X081.

MS 602-4E Shelter Deduction

Clarifies in sub-section 3 that shared shelter costs only need to be verified if questionable. Clarifies in sub-section 6 that SUD should be allowed instead of the homeless shelter deduction if the individual makes a payment for heat because it is more beneficial to the client. Clarifies in sub-section 7 that shelter costs only need to be verified at initial application, during the certification period or at recertification if they are questionable. Adds information in examples that the SUD should be allowed instead of the homeless shelter standard deduction if the individual pays for heat.

MS 604-3E (1) (e) Verification Requirements:Changes Reported During the Certification Period

Clarifies that when a household reports a change in shelter during the certification period, it only needs to be verified if questionable.

MS 605-3 B Expedited Services

Clarifies in sub-section 2 c that shelter costs only need to be verified if questionable.

Previous Section

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2011-03 (04/11)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-03

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: April 1, 2011

We are making several changes to incorporate new policy, clarify existing policy, and align policies within our program manuals. These changes include:

New policy excluding federal income tax returns as income in the month of receipt and as a resource for 12 months from the date of receipt.

Clarification that verification of child support and child care expenses may be postponed for expedite food stamp cases.

Clarification that expedite cases must be processed, and benefits authorized by the 4th day. Previous policy stated that benefits were to be authorized by the 7th day. A change in policy making subsidized adoption payments countable unearned income. This change is a result of a recent clarification from the Food and Nutrition Service.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-5 F When the Application is Delayed

Clarifies that if expedited FS benefits that are not authorized by the 4th day following the application filing date, the application will be considered delayed. Previous policy stated that expedited benefits must be authorized by the 7th day.

MS 602-2 B Excluded Resources

Adds new policy in subsection 31 to explain that federal income tax returns received after December 31, 2009 are an exempt resource for 12 months from the date of receipt. Clarifies that the value of federal income tax returns received within the last 12 months should be deducted from countable liquid resources.

MS 602-2 D Other Household Resources

Removes income tax refunds from Non-recurring Lump Sum Payment section.

MS 602-3 B Unearned Income

Adds subsidized adoption payments to list of countable unearned income.

MS 602-3 D Exempt Income

Removes income tax refunds as a type of non-recurring lump sum payment in sub-section 13. Removes adoption subsidies as a source of exempt income in section 35. Adds new policy in sub-section 35 to explain that federal income tax returns received after December 31, 2009 are excluded from income.

MS 602-4 E (7) Verification of Shelter Costs

Changes the word “or” to “and” in the sentence about recertifications so the wording is consistent with application and certification sentences.

MS 604-2 B (3) Application Filed During the Month Following the Expiration of the Certification Period

Revises section referencing time frame for processing an application to clarify that the expedited FS benefits must be authorized by the 4th day. Previous policy stated that expedited benefits must be authorized by the 7th day.

MS 604-3A(3)(d) Changes That Must Be Acted on for Semi-Annual Reporting

Removes FS E&T non-compliance from disqualification penalty section.

MS 605-1 C Boarders/Foster Care Children and Adults

Changes policy to count subsidized adoption payments as countable income.

MS 605-2 D Income From Self-Employment

Updates the Self-Employment Annualization Standards

MS 605-3(b)(1) Expedited Service Entitlement

Adds a note and example to clarify that federal income tax returns are disregarded when determining a household's eligibility for expedited service. Clarifies that the amount claimed by the household is acceptable verification.

MS 605-3B(2) Verification for Expedited Service Applications

Clarifies that verification of child support and child care expenses may be postponed for expedite food stamp cases.

MS 605-4B(2) Subsistence Hunting and Fishing Statement

Eliminates the requirement for a "signed" Subsistence Hunting and Fishing Statement from the head of household.

MS 605-4B(3) Special ID Card for Subsistence Hunting and Fishing

Eliminates the instructions to enter a Y in the ID CARD REQ field on the FSAD screen.

MS 606-1A(2)(b) Monthly Food Stamp Issuance

Clarifies approval of the EBT unit is required when the EBT card is to be delivered to the office.

MS 606-2 Food Stamp Use at Retailers and Wholesalers

Updates telephone contact information for retailers.

Previous Section

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-04

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: August 1, 2011

We are making several changes to clarify existing policy as a result of feedback from the field. These changes include:

Clarification of the requirement to verify shelter deductions for individuals who live in subsidized housing; Instruction on registering a recertification received the month after it's due; and Clarification explaining when stolen benefits can be replaced.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-4 Deductions

Adds an exception to clarify that an expense must be verified if it results in a deduction and is questionable.

MS 602-4 E Shelter Deduction

Adds a note to section 7 stating that rent for individuals in subsidized housing must be verified.

MS 604-2 B Recertification: Application Filing Deadlines

Adds instruction on registering a recertification received the month after it's due.

MS 604-3 B Status Reporting

Clarifies that new shelter costs must be verified if questionable when a status reporting household reports new shelter expenses.

MS 605-4 B Subsistence Hunting and Fishing

Changes field name from SUBSIST HUNT/FISH to FS SUBSISTENCE ID CARD in second paragraph.

MS 606-1 A Monthly Food Stamp Issuance

Updates EIS Procedure from 2000-10 to 2010-1 Adds a link to EIS Procedure 2010-1

MS 606-3 Replacing Food Stamp Benefits

Changes the title of the 2nd section from “Replacing Benefits Already Spent” to “Replacing Benefits Lost in a Disaster” to more correctly describe this section. Adds a new paragraph to explain that if an Alaska Quest card is reported stolen by a client and benefits were used, only the benefits that were used after the report of loss are eligible for replacement. Adds an example.

Previous Section

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-05

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: September 1, 2011

We are making several changes to incorporate new policy, clarify existing policy, and align policies within our program manuals. These changes include:

The addition of a new military section. All military pay issues are now included in one section and are more fully explained. The addition of Addendum 3 to provide an example of an Leave and Earnings Statement (LES) as well as three tables to define types of Entitlements, Allotments and Deductions that may be found on the LES . Clarification of some PFD issues including expedite cases during PFD season and when to act on FS cases when the PFD is discovered when processing an application for other benefits.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1D U.S. Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Adds a note in subsection 5 clarifying that citizens of the Marshall Islands, Western Samoa, the Federated States of Micronesia and Palau are considered non-immigrants. Adds a row in the table in subsection 6 for Cuban or Haitian Entrants.

MS 602-3 A Earned Income

Removes note on military pay. Adds a link to the new Military Pay section at 605-2 E.

MS 602-3 B Educational Income

Adds that any money that “will not be” used for education expenses is countable unearned income. Previous policy only indicated money that “is not” used for education expenses is countable unearned income. Removes information regarding military pay from subsection 7. This information is now located in Military Pay section 605-2 E. Adds a link to the new Military Pay section 605-2 E.

604-3 A Changes That Must Be Acted On for Semi-Annual Reporting

Adds that a PFD reported by the household on an application for other benefits to the list of changes that must be acted on in subsection 3. Adds that a PFD discovered on an interface when approving an add-on application to the list of changes that must be acted on in subsection 3. Adds an example to subsection 4 explaining processing of an application on which a client reports receipt of a PFD . Adds an example to subsection 4 explaining how to process an application when receipt of a PFD is discovered via an interface. Adds a link to FS MS 605-7B.

604-4 B Adverse Action Notice

Clarifies the count for 10 days adverse action begins the day after the notice is mailed. Removes note about requesting a Fair Hearing. Adds example to further clarify when the 10 day count begins.

605-2 B Excluded Members

Revises example to correct budgeting of an excluded member’s countable income.

605-3 B Expedited Service

Adds link to 605-7 B for information on processing PFD HH benefits in expedite situations.

605-2 E Military Income

Adds a new section to better explain military pay issues.

605-7 B Case Processing With PFD

Adds a bullet to clarify that PFD receipt reported on a new application for other benefits or during an interview must be acted on for FS since it is a change reported by the household. Adds a bullet to clarify that receipt of PFD discovered through an interface when processing a new application for other benefits must be acted on for Food Stamps if the application is approved. Adds a note clarifying processing of expedited PFD Hold Harmless Benefits.

Addendum 3 The Leave and Earnings Statement

Adds an example of an LES received by military personnel. Adds three tables to define Entitlements, Allotments and Deductions that may be listed on the LES .

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updates the standard and shelter deductions. Updates gross and net income limits. Updates the heating and non-heating utility standards. Moves Copper River Census Subarea communities to the Northern utility region to better reflect heating costs in those communities.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2011-06

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: December 1, 2011

This year Social Security announced a 3.6% Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 2012. This manual change updates program policy in conjunction with the COLA increase, which affect the dollar fee limits charged for services performed as a representative payee. These fees are related to recipients of SSI and are subject to increase by the cost-of-living adjustment.

If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-3 B UNEARNED INCOME

Updates the payee fee for SSI recipients due to the COLA increase effective January 2012.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2012-01

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: March 1, 2012

We are making several changes to clarify existing policy as a result of suggestions from the field. We have also made changes to the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines (FPG) for Alaska.

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

Adds a link in subsection 4 to new subsection demonstrating when benefits begin when the 5 year waiting period ends.

Adds a new section to clarify when benefits begin at the end of the 5 year waiting period.

Corrects notice number from X001 to F001. The X001 does not exist.

Clarifies the process for job quit penalties for a semi-annual reporting household in subsection 2 e.

Adds examples to section 2 e.

Clarifies that the same verification rules apply when adding new household members to an existing case that apply at application.

Adds a link to the verification section in 601-4 .

Adds a note to subsection 1 a to clarify how to code TA income and shelter expenses in the household where the adult household members are excluded.

Updates the Annualization Chart based on an increase in federal poverty guidelines.

Clarifies the amount of money used from a joint account by a deployed individual is not countable income to the family in subsection 1 a.

Adds SAVE pay to the list of excluded military income in subsection 2 b ii.

Clarifies that only the amount of military pay provided to the household is countable income in subsection 2 b ii.

Corrects SAVE pay to indicate it is not countable income. This is considered combat pay.

Updates links to current FSP 74 & 75 forms.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2012-02

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: May 18, 2012

The Farm Bill of 2008 (which governs Food Stamp rules) mandated the resource limits for the Food Stamp program increase each year based on the Consumer Price Index. The resource limit for households who are SPECAT and have at least one household member who is disabled or age 60 or older increased to $3,250 effective October 1, 2011. There is no change to the $2,000 resource limit.

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

Increases the resource limit for SPECAT households with at least one member disabled or age 60 or over to $3,250.

Increases the resource limit for SPECAT households with at least one member disabled or age 60 or over to $3,250.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2012-03

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: July 16, 2012

The Food Stamp program allows a deduction for medical expenses for individuals who are elderly or disabled. The Food and Nutrition Service recently clarified that this medical deduction is not allowed for medical marijuana. This manual change updates the Food Stamp manual to include medical marijuana as a medical expense that is not allowed.

If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov.

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

Adds medical marijuana as an expense not allowed as a medical deduction for Food Stamps.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2012-04

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: August 31, 2012

We are making several changes to the Food Stamp manual to incorporate the PFD Hold Harmless manual and PFD Processing Guide into the Food Stamp manual. Information on processing PFD Hold Harmless benefits will now be kept in one place instead of three. There has been no change to PFD Hold Harmless policy.

This change also includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2012.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at (907)465-3328 or email dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 605-7 Permanent Fund Dividend Special Processing

Adds all sections from the former PFD Hold Harmless manual.

Adds additional sections to Food Stamp manual that further detail PFD Hold Harmless processes.

Adds links to examples that open in separate windows.

Adds links EIS processes that open in separate windows.

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updates standards and max shelter deductions.

Updates gross and net income limits.

Updates heating and non-heating utility standards.

Addendum 10 Alaska PFD EIS Notices for PFD Hold Harmless

Creates a new addendum for PFD Hold Harmless notices.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2012-05

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: December 1, 2012

T his year Social Security announced a 1.7% Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 1, 2013. This manual change updates program policy in conjunction with the COLA increase, which affect the dollar fee limits charged for services performed as a representative payee. These fees are related to recipients of SSI and are subject to increase by the cost-of-living adjustment.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at (907)465-3328 or email dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1 D Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Adds subsections 8 and 9 to clarify policy involving citizenship for children born in another country to US citizens and for children who are being adopted as mandated by the "Child Citizenship Act of 2000."

MS 602-3 B(2) Unearned Income - SSI/SSA/VA Income

Updates COLA budget for SSI/SSA/VA income.

MS 604-4 C Notice Requirements During the Certification Period

Adds a number 7 to include adverse action regarding incarcerated household members.

MS 605-1 D Institutions

Updates the contact information for Behavioral Health.

MS 605-2 D Income from Self-Employment

Adds a note containing semi-annual reporting and anticipated income for self-employment households.

MS 605-7 G PFD at Recertification

Adds an example containing suspension versus denial in month before receipt of PFD at recertification.

MS 605-7 K Incorrect Payments

Updates the claim process for PFD Hold Harmless and Food Stamps.

MS 606-3 Replacing Food Stamp Benefits

Adds subtext to further detail replacement benefits in a time of disaster (though, not DSNAP benefits). Includes request by client, process for client and caseworker, and benefit amount replaced. Adds examples.

STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2013-01

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: March 8, 2013

We are making several changes to clarify existing policy as a result of suggestions from the field. We have also made changes to the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines (FPG) for Alaska.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at (907)465-3328 or email: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600- 2 Definitions

Adds an "ice auger" to the list of approved items that can be purchased with Food Stamp benefits for Subsistence Equipment.

Clarifies example 1 to include child under 21 with family that is added to parents Food Stamp case.

Adds an example allowing a child under 21 to have their own Food Stamp case.

Moved example 2 and Note under section (c).

Clarifies that "good faith effort" sale listings include the use of Craigslist, online realtor, newspaper ads, etc.

MS 604 - 3 G Adding a Member to the Household

Adds an example for adding newborns when reported two months before birth.

Adds an example for adding newborns when reported one month before birth.

Adds an example for adding a household member under the age of 21.

Updates self-employment annualization standards with release of Federal Poverty Guidelines for Alaska, effective March 1, 2013.

Department of Health and Social Services

This manual change reflects the updated Fair Hearing regulations regarding how a hearing must be requested and includes a link to the Administrative Manual section 117 for this process.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at (907)465-3328 or email: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

MS 602-1 H Nonfinancial Eligibility

Updates the manual to clarify that the F001 notice is to be sent to all eligible applicants and recertified households who have individuals required to meet work requirements.

MS 602-4 D Medical Deductions

Updates the link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates.

MS 605-1 B Students

Updates the link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates.

MS 605-2 D Income from Self-Employment

Updates the link to the IRS Internal Revenue Service website for current mileage rates.

Updates fair hearing section to read that written and verbal requests for a hearing are acceptable for the Food Stamp program only. Other programs must have a written request.

Removes section "B" detailing "continuation of benefits, " and now refers to Administrative Manual section 117.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2014-04

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: October 1, 2014

The Farm Bill of 2008 (which governs Food Stamp rules) mandated the resource limits for the Food Stamp program increase each year based on the Consumer Price Index. The limits for SPECAT households were increased to $3250 in 2011. We received notice September 8, 2014, that the resource limits for other Food Stamp cases will increase to $2250 effective October 1, 2014. There is no change to the resource limit for other programs.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at 465-3382 or e-mail dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

602-2 Resources

Increases the resource limit for households to $2,250.

604-3 Status Reporting

Increases the resource limit for households to $2,250.

Addendum 4

Increases the resource limit for households to $2,250.

This year Social Security announced a 1.7% Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 1, 2015. STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2015-05

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: December 1, 2015

This update adds Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents ( ABAWD ) policy that takes effect January 1, 2016. This includes new definitions, work requirements and work exemptions, countable months, exempt and non-exempt areas, and several new examples.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at email: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 Definitions

Adds definition for dependent child, countable month, and non-countable month.

MS 602-1F Work Requirements

Adds the ABAWD work requirements.

MS 602-1G Determining Requirements and Exemptions

Adds the ABAWD work exemptions.

Adds a note to clarify comparable disqualification for Food Stamps and TANF .

MS 602-1L Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents

Adds the ABAWD policy that includes countable and non-countable months, household composition, work requirements, work exemptions, time limits, and reporting requirements.

Adds examples through out policy section.

MS 604-3A Semi-Annual Reporting

Adds statement to clarify what an ABAWD must report.

MS 605 - 3B Expedited Service

Adds note to clarify how to code ABAWD status when status is unknown at time of authorization.

Addendum 2 ABAWD Work Requirement Exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Previously reserved, adds exempt and non-exempt ABAWD waiver areas.

Addendum 8 Communities by Census Area

Corrects utility regions areas for Bristol Bay, Dillingham, and Lake and Peninsula census areas.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2016-03

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: August 1, 2016

This manual change primarily reflects the change in State Statute regarding the treatment of drug felons and Alaska's ABAWD exemption.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team via email at: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4 A . Verification Requirements

Updated the note for eDRS checks as Eligibility Technicians in the field offices, not Field Services, now complete these verifications. The note was updated to reflect current procedure in the field.

Changed the name of the Department of Labor interface to SAM and added employment history through quarterly wage match to the information verified section.

Added eDRS to the Interfaces that need to be checked under certain conditions in 601-4 A.(3)

MS 602-1 A. Households

Added probation or parole violators to the excluded household members section.

Added four clauses under drug felon that would allow a drug felon to be eligible for SNAP benefits.

Updated the note to align with the ATAP manual and include a cross reference to the Administrative Procedures Manual section for verification. Change based on a change in Alaska State Statute and in accordance with federal regulation.

M S 602-1 B . Residency

Removed dpafield email address for eDRS checks when a client moves to Alaska.

MS 602-1 F . Work Requirements

Updated ABAWD work requirements to add information about "Workfare" and volunteering at participating non-profit organizations to maintain or regain SNAP eligibility.

MS 602-1 L . ABAWD's

Added a note and a bullet to clarify that all of Alaska is currently exempt from the ABAWD work requirements and time limitations through May 31st of 2017.

Updated ABAWD work requirements to add information about "Workfare" and volunteering at participating non-profit organizations.

Added bullet that Workfare Program activities can help an individual regain eligibility.

Added section to clarify certification periods for households containing ABAWD's.

MS 602-2 A . Definition and Types of Resources

Added language for how to treat Crowdfunding resources based on FNS guidance dated 01/11/2016.

MS 602-3 C . Other Income

Updated drug felon language to state "ineligible drug felon", rather than just "drug felon". Change based on a change in Alaska State Statute.

MS 604-1 A . Length of Certification Period

Added information regarding the certification period for ABAWD 's.

MS 604-2 E . Verification Requirements: Recertification

Added that IPV status must be checked when a household member is added.

Changed the name of the Department of Labor interface to SAM and added employment history through quarterly wage match to the information verified section.

Added eDRS to list of interfaces that must be checked.

MS 604-3 E . Verification Requirements: Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Added bullet that IPV status needs to be checked if a household member moves in.

MS 604-3 G . Adding a Member to the Household

Updated the note for eDRS checks as Eligibility Technicians in the field offices, not Field Services, now complete these verifications. The note was updated to reflect current procedure in the field.

MS 605-2 B . Excluded Members

Updated drug felon language to state "ineligible drug felon", rather than just"drug felon". Change based on SB91.

MS 605-2 D. (11) Policy Differences Between Temporary Assistance, Family Medicaid and Food Stamps

Removed this subsection due to outdated policy, program names and hyperlinks.

MS 605-3 A . Quarterly or Semiannual Issuances

Removes obsolete wording regarding quarterly and semi-annual issuances in certain rural areas.

Addendum 2 ABAWD Work Requirement exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Changed the Municipality of Anchorage from a non-exempt area to an exempt area for ABAWD work requirements and time limits as of June 1, 2016 based on FNS 's approval of ABAWD modification waiver.

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Corrected typo for South Central electric deduction amount.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2016-04

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: October 3, 2016

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2016.

Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development Team via email at: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-3(B ) Unearned Income

Added clarification of income budgeting for recoupments and mandatory payees from SSI/SSA/VA income.

MS 602-3(D) Exempt Income

Updated Workforce Investment Act (WIA) to Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2014 (WIOA).

MS 604-3(G) Adding a member to the household

Clarified section regarding adding a new household member or child when reported the month before they join the household.

Removed an example that included information about reporting a new household member two months prior to their birth.

MS 605-1(B) Students

Updated WIA Workforce Investment Act to WIOA Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2014.

A dded mandatory training and on the job (OTJ) training as an exclusion to student status.

MS 605-2(E) Military Pay

Clarified and reorganized the countable military earned income section. Distinguished between countable earned income paid to families living off base and exempt meals provided on base to military personnel.

MS 605-3(B) Expedited Services

Added language to clarify that the waiver should not be applied if we’re out of time frames due to the 20% limitation established by FNS.

C larified that SNAP benefits are issued on the fourth day in the example to ensure the field understands we use the waiver to stay within time frames.

Addendum 2 ABAWD work Requirement exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Updated the “WADE HAMPTON CENSUS AREA” to “KUSILVAK CENSUS AREA”.

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the “WADE HAMPTON CENSUS AREA” to “KUSILVAK CENSUS AREA” in the Southwestern Utility Region.

Updated the FFY 17 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS.

Addendum 7 Budget Work Sheets

Updated the links to the FSP74 and FSP75 with updated standards information based on Addendum 4.

Addendum 8 Communities by Census Area

Updated the “WADE HAMPTON CENSUS AREA” to “KUSILVAK CENSUS AREA”.

Addendum 9 Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Updated the list of approved substance abuse treatment centers with Teen Challenge Pacific NW Adult and Teen Alaska CA due to their recent FNS approval.

Renamed Addendum 9 "Approved Substance Abuse Programs" and removed the word "State" from the title.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2016-5

MANUAL: Alaska Food Stamp Program

DATE: December 1, 2016

This year Social Security announced a 0.3% Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 1, 2017. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at (907)465-3328 or email dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1C IDENTITY

Removed SVES (State Verification Exchange System) as a verification source for identity.

Added information about accepting a Medicaid determination as acceptable verification for identity. Change based on several inquiries and a FNS Policy announcement from 3/18/2014 titled, “SNAP and ACA Parts I and II Memo.”

· Added a hyperlink to MAGI MS 808-6 F. – Proof of Identity Only.

MS 602-2 B EXEMPT RESOURCES

· Added ABLE accounts to list of excluded resources for SNAP.

MS 604-3(A) SEMI-ANNUAL REPORTING

· Added clarification to semi-annual reporting section that all changes reported on any case should be worked for SNAP cases.

MS 605-2(D) CASES REQUIRING SPECIALIZED PROCEDURES

· Removed language from the first example in section 605-2(D.)(6.)(b.) that implies income can be prospected based on draws or advances. Change made based on a policy clarification previously sent and manual not updated – emails dated 10/20/16 and 12/29/15.

MS 605-7(F) PFD AT APPLICATION

· Removed the term Family Medicaid and replaced it with MAGI Medicaid.

ADDENDUM 1 CITY AND VILLAGE CLASSIFICATION

Updated the city of Barrow with their new name, “Utqiagvik.”

ADDENDUM 2 ABAWD WORK REQUIREMENT EXEMPT & NON-EXEMPT

· Updated the city of Barrow with their new name, “Utqiagvik.”

ADDENDUM 5 LIST OF INCOME AND RESOURCES EXCLUDED BY OTHER FEDERAL LAWS

Updated list of Native funds that are excluded by Federal Law.

Re-Organized addendum so that list of income and resources excluded by other federal laws now reads from most recent to oldest.

ADDENDUM 8 LIST OF COMMUNITIES BY CENSUS AREAS

· Updated the city of Barrow with their new name, “Utqiagvik.”

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2017-01

MANUAL: Alaska SNAP

DATE: April 3, 2017

We renamed this manual to reflect the federal name change from The Food Stamp Program to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program with the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008. We have made changes to the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines ( FPG Federal Poverty Guidelines ) for Alaska. We also updated the effective dates of Alaska's statewide waiver from the ABAWD work requirements and time limits. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team via email at: dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601- 4 A. VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS FOR INITIAL APPLICATIONS

Added information about secondary verification when eDRS shows an IPV.

Removed recertification from eDRS section of table a. Interfaces that must be checked

MS 602-1 G.(2)(i) DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Removed language regarding TANF recipients being disqualified from SNAP when they are under a TANF work penalty

MS 602-1 G.(3)(a) DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Added the Tribal TANF programs of: Maniilaq, BBNA KANA to the list of tribal organizations that provide TANF benefits and may impose penalties.

Removed work requirement wording from the reason that TANF may be reduced.

MS 602-1 L. ABLE-BODIED ADULTS WITHOUT DEPENDENTS

Updated effective dates of ABAWD waiver

MS 602-2 B. EXEMPT RESOURCES

Added list of Retirement accounts that are excluded from resources by the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008.

Fixed ABLE expandable term

MS 602-2 E. TRANSFER OF RESOURCES

Corrected the calculation in example in number #2.

MS 602-4 E. DEDUCTIONS

Updated with information for reviewing heating assistance awards (both State and Native Organizations) prior to making an eligibility determination.

Add link to LIHEAP award list on DPAweb.

MS 604-2 E.(2)(a) VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: RECERTIFICATION

Removed recertification from eDRS section of table a. Interfaces that must be checked

MS 605-2 D. INCOME FROM SELF-EMPLOYMENT

Updated self-employment annualization standards with release of Federal Poverty Guidelines for Alaska, effective April 1, 2017.

ADDENDUM 2 ABAWD WORK REQUIREMENT EXEMPT & NON-EXEMPT

Updated effective dates of the statewide ABAWD waiver.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2017-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 1, 2017

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2017. Additionally, the resource limit for SPECAT households was increased from $3,250 to $3,500. All manual sections have been updated to use the term SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) in place of Food Stamps. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy and Program Development Team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-5 F. When the Application is Delayed

Added new section 601-5 F. (a.) regarding agency caused delays over 1 year

Added a note to clarify that the 12-month “look-back” period begins on, “…the date on which the State agency is notified, or otherwise discovers that a loss to a household has occurred”

MS 602-1 A. Households

Updated examples for consistency that a child under the age of 22 must be included on their parents SNAP case

Clarified example that a foster child may be included in the foster parent or biological parents household, but the unearned income that is received (foster care subsidy, SSI, RSDI, etc.) for the care of the child must be counted if the child is included

MS 602-2 Resources

Updated the resource limit for SPECAT households to $3500.00 per SNAP Fiscal Year 2018 Cost-of Living Adjustments

MS 602-2 A.(1)(c) Definitions and Types of Resources

Added section that clarifies the policy regarding credit card company gift cards

MS 602-2 D. Other Household Resources

Added to exceptions: “Establishment specific gift cards are not treated as a resource in the month of receipt”

MS 602-3 B. Unearned Income

Added subsection (s) in number 7 that clarifies credit card company gift cards may be countable unearned income if they are regularly received and easily anticipated

Updated and corrected the numbering conventions in subsections 2, 3, 4, and 6 from numerical to alphabetical

MS 602-3 D.(13)&(38) Exempt Income

Added an additional exception to the lump sum policy in section 13 that clarifies FNS’ policy that store specific gift card balances are not counted towards the resource test

Added new section 38 which clarifies that store specific gift cards are treated as non-recurring lump sum payments and exempt as income and as a resource

MS 604-3 B. Status Reporting

Added a note clarifying that APA clients in refused cash status are considered APA recipients

ADDENDUM 4 Program Standards & Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2018 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

Updated the resource limit for SPECAT households to $3500.00 per SNAP Fiscal Year 2018 Cost-of Living Adjustments
