STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2017-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2017

This update expanded and clarified information regarding identity verification and aligned the SNAP qualified alien policy with other manuals. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at d papolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 DEFINITIONS

Corrected link to Federal minimum wage.

MS 602-1 C. IDENTITY

Expanded and clarified information regarding identity verification.

MS 602-1 D. US CITIZENSHIP AND ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Aligned qualified alien policy and people exempt from “5-Year” waiting period with other manuals

MS 605-3B EXPEDITED SERVICE

C larified that postponed interviews are treated the same as other postponed verifications. If the client's interview is postponed and they "no-show" for the scheduled interview, they cannot have their interview postponed again.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2018-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 2, 2018

This update revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines ( FPG Federal Poverty Guidelines ) for Alaska and removed references to status reporting as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at d papolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-3(A) WHEN IS AN INTERVIEW REQUIRED

Added information that households meeting the elderly / disabled FNS waiver requirements may be exempt from the recertification interview requirement Clarified that an interview does not need to be completed if a household submits their recertification after the end of the certification period but within the following month and is not due for their annual interview

MS 601-3(F) CHANGES REPORTED DURING THE INTERVIEW

Removed references to status reporting households as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018

MS 601-5(C) PENDING THE APPLICATION

Clarified that an interview does not need to be completed if a household submits their recertification after the end of the certification period but within the following month and is not due for their annual interview

MS 602-1(A) HOUSEHOLDS

Clarified household composition rules regarding homeless shelters Added majority custody language to manual due to a fair hearing loss Added clarification that proof of compliance with drug felon criteria is required Added explanation of the four-part test to establish fleeing felon status

MS 602-1(I) VOLUNTARY JOB QUIT AND REDUCTION OF WORK HOURS

Removed references to status reporting households as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018

MS 602-2(A) DEFINITION AND TYPES OF RESOURCES

Added clarification regarding crowdfunding accounts

MS 604-1(A) LENGTH OF CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Removed references to status reporting households as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018 Clarified circumstances in which shorter certification periods can be provided

MS 604-2 RECERTIFICATION

Added information regarding the new FNS waiver allowing Alaska to waive the recertification interview for certain elderly / disabled households Clarified that an interview does not need to be completed if a household submits their recertification after the end of the certification period but within the following month and is not due for their annual interview

MS 604-3 REPORTING AND ACTING ON CHANGES DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Removed references to status reporting households as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018

MS 604-3(G) PUBLIC ASSISTANCE INCOME CHANGES

Reworded section to clarify that SNAP overpayments are worked with the amount of TANF that should have been issued unless fraud is involved

MS 605-1(D) INSTITUTIONS

Updated section DAC/GLA rules to match FCEA 2008 and new reporting requirements at 7 CFR 273.11(e) and 7 CFR 273.11(f)

MS 605-2(D)7 ANNUALIZATION DETERMINATION

Updated self-employment annualization standards with release of the Federal Poverty Guidelines for Alaska, effective April 1, 2018

MS 605-3(B) EXPEDITED SERVICE

Clarified information regarding the postponed interview waiver Added examples illustrating when and when not to use the waiver outside of the expedite timeframes

MS 605-7(I) PFD REPORTING REQUIREMENTS

Removed references to status reporting households as there is no longer status reporting in Alaska effective April 1, 2018

ADDENDUM 1

Added Joint Base Elmendorf Richardson (JBER) to the list of urban cities

ADDENDUM 8

Added Joint Base Elmendorf Richardson (JBER) to the list of communities by census area for Anchorage

ADDENDUM 9

Added Valley Oaks Residential Treatment Facility to the list of approved substance abuse facilities Added Phillips Ayagnirvik Treatment Center to the list of approved substance abuse facilities

ADDENDUM 10

Deleted F194 as this notice is no longer active as of April 1, 2018

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2018-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: July 2, 2018

This update clarified caseworker responsibilities when there is a work required SNAP household member and replaced the term "semi-annual" with "simplified reporting" throughout the manual. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at d papolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1(H) CASEWORKER RESPONSIBILITIES - WORK REQUIREMENTS

Clarified that the F001 must be sent at application and recertification when there is a work required SNAP household member, anytime a previously exempt individual becomes subject to work requirements, and anytime a work required household member moves into the SNAP household

MS 602-2(A) DEFINITION AND TYPES OF RESOURCES

Clarified that loans, other than deferred repayment educational loans, are counted as a liquid resources the month after receipt if the money is retained as cash or in a bank account

MS 602-2(B)24 EXEMPT RESOURCES

Clarified that in order for resources held by recipients of TANF, SSI, or APA to be exempt, they must be solely owned by the TANF, SSI, or APA recipient

MS 602-3(B)6&7 UNEARNED INCOME

Clarified that BIA GA payments made to the household or to a vendor for the household are considered countable unearned income unless received after the month of intended use

MS 602-3(D)30 EXEMPT INCOME

Added note to clarify that BIA GA payments made to the household or to a vendor for the household are considered countable unearned income unless received after the month of intended use. Only BIA Burial Assistance payments made to a vendor are exempt.

MS 602-4(D) MEDICAL DEDUCTION

Replaced link to IRS Standard Mileage Rates in 602-4(D)1(h) for the current information

MS 604-3 REPORTING AND ACTING ON CHANGES DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Clarified that an EBT transaction report showing out of state usage may be an indicator that a household moved out of state but should not be used as the only basis for closure. We must request proof of residency.

MS 605-1(B) STUDENTS

Revised student eligibility section to match federal regulations for clarity

STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2018-04

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 3, 2018

This update added information regarding interfaces that can be used to verify residency. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at d papolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-5(D) DENYING THE APPLICATION

Clarified that when denying an application for failure to provide, the denial should take place the day after the due date, not the day of

MS 602-1(B) RESIDENCY

Clarified which available interfaces may also be used to verify residency and when

MS 602-2(B) EXEMPT RESOURCES

Added a description of what a utility trailer is as this general term is causing confusion

M S 602-3(B) UNEARNED INCOME

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change

MS 604-2(C) RECERTIFICATION: CASEWORKER ACTIONS

Added required notices to be sent for recertifications that waived the interview due to the elderly/disabled households waiver

MS 605-1(D) INSTITUTIONS

Clarified which institutions allow SNAP recipients to receive benefits while residing in an institution that provides more than 50% of meals

MS 605-3(B) EXPEDITE SERVICE

Clarified that in situations where verification was postponed for an expedite application, overpayments are only processed if the household misrepresented their income or the caseworker incorrectly applied policy

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2019-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2019

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines effective April 1, 2019. This update also adds information indicating that California will no longer provide additional cash for food as part of their SSI state supplement as of June 1, 2019. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at d papolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-1(C) SSI RECIPIENTS FROM CALIFORNIA

Added information indicating California residents will no longer be receiving additional cash for food as part of their SSI state supplement effective June 1, 2019

MS 602-1(A) HOUSEHOLDS

Clarified that if regularly employed household members are in the home for at least part of the calendar month, then household member would be included in SNAP household

MS 602-1(L) ABLE-BODIED ADULTS WITHOUT DEPENDENTS

Updated ABAWD exemption effective dates to January 1, 2017 through December 31, 2019

M S 602-3(B) UNEARNED INCOME

Clarified that income diverted to an ex-spouse under the Uniformed Services Former Spouse Protection Act is excluded from the SNAP benefit calculation of the military retiree

M S 602-4(E) SHELTER DEDUCTION

Revised the homeless shelter deduction from $143 to $147 effective April 1, 2019

MS 604-1(B) NOTICES OF EXPIRATION OF CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Revised the notice title from Food Stamps to SNAP for the X004 and F801

MS 605-1(B) STUDENTS

Clarified that for 2 parent households, it must be determined which parent provides care for a child under the age of 6 to be considered an eligible student Clarified student eligibility due to performing self-employment for 20 hours per week and receiving weekly earnings at least equal to the federal minimum wage. Use self-employment earnings prior to allowable self-employment deductions.

MS 605-2(D) INCOME FROM SELF-EMPLOYMENT

Updated Self-Employment Annualization Standards due to FPL change effective 4/1/19

ADDENDUM 5 LIST OF INCOME AND RESOURCES EXCLUDED BY OTHER FEDERAL LAWS

Removed #18 which was referencing P.L. 100-241 (Section 15) as ANCSA dividends are exempt income and have been for years

ADDENDUM 10 ALASKA PERMANENT FUND DIVIDEND EIS NOTICES FOR PFD HOLD HARMLESS

Revised the notice titles from Food Stamps to SNAP for the F107, F117, F190, F192, and F193

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2019-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 3, 2019

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2019. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4 VERIFICATION

Clarified that certain types of verification are considered "verified upon receipt" as the information provided is not questionable, the provider is the primary source of the information, and no further information is needed to take action.

MS 601-5(B) BENEFIT START DATE

Clarified that applications from incarcerated individuals must be denied. We cannot adjust the benefit start date to the date of release or the first of the next month

MS 602-1(A) HOUSEHOLDS

Clarified individuals who are age 60 or older who are unable to purchase food and prepare meals with others in the household because of a permanent disability may not be considered a separate household to children in the home in which they are considered the caretaker relative

Added that ex-felons convicted of aggravated sexual abuse, murder, sexual exploitation and abuse of children, or sexual assault who have served their sentence, are prohibited from participating in SNAP unless they comply with the terms of their parole

MS 602-1(F) WORK REQUIREMENTS

Revised due to ABAWD requirements expected in the near future

M S 602-1(G) DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Clarified the difference between SNAP benefit reductions of 25% due to ATAP work requirement penalty and a SNAP disqualification

Revised section on ABAWD's for clarity due to ABAWD requirements expected in the near future

M S 602-1(L) ABLE-BODIED ADULTS WITHOUT DEPENDENTS (ABAWD's)

Revised due to ABAWD requirements expected in the near future

MS 602-2(B) EXEMPT RESOURCES

Clarified income producing property and when it can be used as a resource exemption

Clarified resources of SSI recipients are no longer considered exempt if the SSI recipient is ineligible for SNAP due to a drug related felony

MS 602-2(D) OTHER HOUSEHOLD RESOURCES

Added that households contain individuals with significant lottery or gaming winnings($25,000 or more won in a single game) are ineligible for SNAP benefits until they again meet normal income and resource standards.

MS 602-3(B) UNEARNED INCOME

Clarified when SNAP benefit is not reduced when there is no ATAP or NFAP benefit issued in the month.

Added that households contain individuals with significant lottery or gaming winnings($25,000 or more won in a single game) are ineligible for SNAP benefits until they again meet normal income and resource standards.

MS 605-1(B) STUDENTS

Added that participants enrolled in school as a requirement of their SNAP Employment and Training plan are eligible for SNAP if they are enrolled in career and technical education, remedial courses, basic adult education, literacy instruction, or English as a second language

MS 605-1(D) INSTITUTIONS

Revised drug and alcohol treatment center responsibilities due to a final rule published by FNS.

M S 605-2(B) EXCLUDED MEMBERS

Added ex-felons convicted of aggravated sexual abuse, murder, sexual exploitation and abuse of children, or sexual assault who have served their sentence are excluded members

C larified resources of SSI recipients are no longer considered exempt if the SSI recipient is ineligible for SNAP due to a drug related felony

MS 605-3(B) EXPEDITED SERVICE

Removed SNAP notice F154 as this notice is no longer used

ADDENDUM 2 ABAWD WORK REQUIREMENT EXEMPT AND NON-EXEMPT AREAS

Revised ABAWD 36-month period due to ABAWD requirements expected in the near future

ADDENDUM 4 SNAP STANDARDS AND MAXIMUM ALLOTMENTS

Updated the FFY 2020 maximum allotments and utility standards

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2019-04

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 2, 2019

This year the Social Security Administration announced a Cost of Living Allowance ( COLA ) increase effective January 2020, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income. In addition to the COLA change, this manual revision includes an ABAWD policy clarification regarding who is considered physically or mentally unfit for work as a result of guidance from FNS .

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 602-1(A) Households

Clarified that if an individual has multiple drug felony convictions that occurred on or after August 22, 1996, and did not meet one or more of the conditions listed in SNAP MS 602-1(A)(5)(g) for even just one of the convictions, the individual would be ineligible for SNAP

Clarified that if a client had multiple sexual abuse, murder, sexual exploitation etc. felony convictions that occurred on or after February 7, 2014, and did not meet one or more of the conditions listed in SNAP MS 602-1(A)(5)(h) for even just one of the convictions, the client would be ineligible for SNAP

MS 602-1(D) US Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Clarified that individuals who are approved for Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals ( DACA ) are considered non-qualified aliens

MS 602-1(G) Determining Work Requirements and Exemptions

Clarified anyone attending high school at least half-time is considered an exempt student EIS Code I

I ncluded chronic homelessness as an ABAWD exemption under persons physically or mentally unfit for employment, rather than a separate exemption type

MS 602-1(I) Voluntary Job Quit and Reduction of Work Hours

Clarified if a participating household voluntarily reports a job quit and it is determined that good cause does not exist, we would take action at that time and give them notice of adverse action. If the job quit is discovered by other means, no action is taken until the household recertifies

Clarified when expedite SNAP benefits are issued, the household is considered a participating household and we would follow the participating household policy when applying job quit penalties

MS 602-1(L) Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents (ABAWDs)

Clarified homelessness is an exemption for ABAWD . Chronic homelessness was not previously added to this section

Updated example to change Wasilla to exempt and Anchorage to non-exempt

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change

MS 602-3(D) Exempt Income

Added 2020 Census income due to a demonstration waiver approved by FNS

MS 602-4(D) Medical Deduction

Clarified that a medical deduction is not allowed for Medicare Part B premiums while we are waiting for the buy-in to kick in. Medicare Part B premiums are a reimbursable expense

MS 604-3(D) Temporary Ineligibility/Case Suspension

Clarified for timely recertifications, if the household is ineligible for SNAP benefits in the month immediately following the end of its current certification period, the recertification is denied

C larified that PFD is an exception to this rule if the household is eligible in the following month

MS 605-1(D) Institutions

Clarified institutions include hospitals, boarding schools, military schools, Job Corps, nursing homes, mental health facilities, and prisons

MS 605-3(B) Expedited Service

Clarified in determining expedite eligibility, we should use the client reported rent and the standard heating or non-heating utility standards for their region (if higher than the actual reported utility expenses) and compare this total with their reported countable monthly gross income and liquid resources

MS 605-4(B) Subsistence Hunting and Fishing

Clarified that subsistence hunting and fishing requires a written statement signed by the head of household, spouse, or authorized representative that they intend to secure a substantial portion of their food by means of subsistence hunting and fishing. The statement must also indicate that they do not intend to use the items purchased for commercial purposes

MS 608-1 Fair Hearings

Linked fair hearing procedures to Administrative Procedures Manual at section 117-2

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2020-01 (04/20)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2020-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2020

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines effective April 1, 2020. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 Definitions

Clarified that volunteering at a community service site is considered work

MS 601-2(A) What Is an Acceptable Application Form?

Added that Senior Benefits will accept the GEN 72 to determine continued eligibility. This change is effective with the April 2020 form revision

MS 601-4(A) Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Clarified eDRS only needs to be checked at the time of application or when there is a newly added household member.

MS 602-1(A) Households

Clarified that individuals must meet one of the qualifying conditions for each felony conviction, or they are ineligible for SNAP

Clarified what convictions fall in the category of "sexual exploitation and abuse of children"

MS 602-1(E) Social Security Numbers

Clarified that if a newborn's SSN is not provided at the time of application, the SSN or proof of application for a SSN is required at recertification or six months following the newborn's birth, whichever is later.

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Revised policy to allow anyone 16 and older to volunteer for E&T

Added a list of current E&T providers in Alaska

MS 602-1(G) Determining Work Requirements and Exemptions

Clarified that individuals must be participating in the subsistence hunting and fishing purchase provisions and be engaged in subsistence hunting and/or fishing an average of at least 30 hours weekly during the certification period to be eligible for the subsistence hunting and fishing work exemption.

Clarified that individuals who reside in Anchorage may participate in E&T. Kenai and Fairbanks DPA offices ceased providing E&T services on October 1, 2019.

Changed title of MS 602-1G(2) from "Individuals Exempt from Work Requirements" to "Individuals Exempt from Basic Work Requirements" for clarity

MS 602-1(H) Caseworker Responsibilities - Work Requirements

Removed item # 3 as there is no longer an active referral process for E&T as of February 2020

Renumbered section

MS 602-1(I) Work Requirement Disqualification Procedures

Clarified a job quit disqualification can only be deleted if the penalty hasn't started yet. Once started, it will only end early if the person becomes exempt from work requirements.

Changed title from "Work Requirement Disqualification Procedures" to "Restoring Benefits" for clarity

MS 602-1(L) Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents (ABAWDs)

Rewrote section for clarity

Clarified when non-reportable change occurs and is not reported that causes an ABAWD required individual to lose ABAWD exemption, we must retroactively re-calculate ABAWD months

Clarified what is required for Native and Tribal Corporation programs to be considered an approved work program

Clarified ABAWD months are considered countable until we can verify the individual is meeting ABAWD work requirements or is exempt

Clarified a SNAP household that contains a child under the age of 18 would be considered exempt from ABAWD work requirements regardless of caretaker responsibility

Clarified when overpayment are processed and when they are not

Added volunteering at a community service site as an acceptable way to meet ABAWD work requirements

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Clarified payee fee policy applies to SSI and other SSA benefits, as long as the payee was approved by the Social Security Administration

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Updated homeless shelter deduction from $147 to $152

MS 605-2(D) Income From Self-Employment

Updated Self-Employment Annualization Standard due to FPL change effective 4/1/20

MS 607-1 Calculating the Correct Benefit

Clarified when correcting benefits, actuals would only be used to determine the corrected benefit if the household did not report income as required, or a caseworker did not include reported income

Changed title of 607-1A from "Correcting Income Errors" to "Income Errors" for clarity

MS 607-2 Restored Benefits

Clarified calculations for lost benefits are determined up to the date the error was corrected or the end of the certification period, whichever occurs first

Changed title from "Restored Benefits" to "Correcting Errors That Result in an Underpayment (Restored Benefits)" for clarity

MS 607-3 Claims

Added a link directing to SNAP MS 604-3(G) that explains how to count TA and APA overpayments when processing SNAP overpayments

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2020-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 1, 2020

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2020. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-2(D) What is an Identifiable Application?

Clarified that a post office deliverable address (residential or mailing) is required for an application to be considered identifiable

MS 601-3(C) What Can Conduct the Interview?

Clarified that a NFAP agency not allowed to conduct a SNAP interview

MS 601-3(E) Scheduling Interviews

Clarified that sending an EIS notice informing the applicant of their interview date and time is only required if the applicant has not agreed to a scheduled interview during a phone conversation or been provided with a printout from appointment plus or the "What do I need to bring to my interview?" sheet in the GEN 50C

MS 602-1(A) Households

Clarified what verifications can be used to verify that a conviction falls under U.S. Code Title 18 Chapter 10

MS 602-2(B) Exempt Resources

Clarified that money held in an approved Medicaid Qualifying Trust does not count as a resource to the individual

MS 602-2(D) Other Household Resources

Added substantial lottery and game winnings policy as required by the Farm Bill

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Added substantial lottery and gaming winnings policy as required by the Farm Bill Clarified that income deposited directly into an approved Medicaid Qualifying Trust does not count as income to the individual

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Updated the homeless shelter deduction from $152 to $157

MS 604-2 Recertification

Clarified that a GEN 50C or GEN 72 is received, it must be used to establish a new certification period for all open programs

MS 604-3 Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Added substantial lottery and gaming winnings as a change that must be reported and acted on as required by the Farm Bill

MS 605-3(B) Expedited Service

Removed information regarding the Postpone Interview Waiver as this is no longer allowed by FNS

MS 607-1 Calculating the Correct Benefit

Updated Example 1 to reflect 10-10-10 and adverse action rules that still apply when processing changes

MS 608-1 Fair Hearings

Clarified if the household requests for a fair hearing before the effective date of action, we should be continuing benefits automatically unless the household indicates that they do not want continued benefits

Addendum 2 ABAWD Work Requirement Exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Added Marshall under Kusilvak Census Area

Addendum 4 SNAP Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2021 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

Addendum 9 Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Updated the phone number for the Salvation Army Adult Rehabilitation Program Changed the name of Haven House to Sitka Counseling and updated their address

Addendum 10 Alaska Permanent Fund Dividend EIS Notice for PFD Hold Harmless

Removed postponed interview notices

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2020-03 (12/20)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2020-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2020

This year the Social Security Administration announced a Cost of Living Allowance ( COLA ) increase effective January 2021, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Clarified that in order to determine eligibility for SNAP, an interview must be conducted by a DPA caseworker or contracted fee agent. A NFAP interview cannot be used as an interview for SNAP.

MS 601-4 Verification

Clarified that an eligibility determination cannot be based solely on the information provided on an application or recertification. The caseworker must confirm that the household is ineligible before the application or recertification can be denied. Verbal confirmation of ineligibility is sufficient.

Clarified that verification of shelter costs are required.

Added information regarding the Instant Eligibility Verification System (IEVS).

Changed in child support interface name from NSTAR to WINSTAR .

MS 602-1(C) Identity

Clarified there is no requirement that the document used to verify identity include a current address.

Added SOLQ as an acceptable verification source for identity.

Clarified that existing records retained in ILINX can continue to be used as verification of identity.

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Revised to include the new E&T providers "My House" and "Covenant House".

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change.

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Clarified verification of shelter costs are required.

MS 604-2(E) Recertification

Changed child support interface name from NSTAR to WINSTAR .

Added information regarding the Instant Eligibility Verification System (IEVS).

MS 605-1(D) Institutions

Clarified residents of FNS or State Approved treatment centers can only participate in SNAP using the authorized representative designated by the center.

Added link to the SNAP Authorized Representative EBT Card job aid.

Addendum 1 SNAP Program City and Village Classification

Added Marshall in Rural II as it is not currently listed.

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2021-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2021

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines effective April 1, 2021. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

H ighlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Added information regarding acceptable application signatures

MS 601-2(A) What Is an Acceptable Application Form?

Clarified that a Native Family Assistance Program (NFAP) application form is an acceptable application and recertification form

MS 601-2(D) What Is an Identifiable Application?

Added link to application signature requirements, section 601

MS 601-2(E) Who Can Sign the Application?

Added link to application signature requirements, section 601

MS 601-4(A) Verification

Removed reference to questionable shelter costs in subsection 2, as verification of shelter costs is required

MS 601-5(E) Withdrawing the Application

Added link to application signature requirements, section 601

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Removed My House from the list of SNAP E&T providers

MS 602-2(B) Excluded Resources

Added subsection regarding Dingle Act Lands

Renumbered section

MS 602-3(B) Countable Vendor Payments

Clarified when a vendor payment is counted as income, exempt as income, and allowable as a shelter deduction

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Removed examples of questionable shelter cost situations as verification of shelter costs is required

MS 604-2 Recertification

Added link to application signature requirements, section 601

MS 604-2(A) Recertification Application Form

Clarified a Native Family Assistance Program (NFAP) application form is an acceptable application and recertification form

MS 604-3 Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Revised to include the PARIS match

MS 604-3(G) Public Assistance Income Changes

Linked SNAP MS 604-3(G) to SNAP MS 607 so this overpayment calculation policy doesn't continue to be overlooked

MS 605-2(B) Counting Resources and Budgeting Income and Expenses

Clarified that ATAP benefits are counted toward the children when coded "DI"

MS 605-2(D) Annualization Determination

Updated the Self-Employment Standards to reflect updated FPL effective April 2021

MS 607-3(E) Establishing An Overpayment Claim

Clarified how far back to calculate IPV claims

Addendum 2 ABAWD Work Requirement Exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Updated Alaska's Census Areas due to an update with the US Census Bureau

Addendum 4 Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated Alaska's Census Areas due to an update with the US Census Bureau

Addendum 8 List of Communities by Census Areas

Updated Alaska's Census Areas due to an update with the US Census Bureau

Addendum 9 State & FNS Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Added Alaska Dream Center and Set Free Alaska as they are now FNS approved treatment centers

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2021-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 1, 2021

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2021. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at dpapolicy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4(A) Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Clarified regulations do not allow us to pend for verification of expenses unless it affects the household's eligibility or allotment level

Removed INGENS as INGENS will no longer be accessible to eligibility workers

MS 601-5(D) Actions Taken on the Application

Clarified verbal client statement can verify a household is over the SNAP income or resource limit. We cannot deny the SNAP application solely based on information listed in the SNAP application

MS 602-1(A) Excluded Household Members

Added information that indicates when verifying drug felony information, the Felony Verification Form must be used and submitted to the Anchorage Parole Office for completion

MS 602-1(D) US Citizenship and Eligibile Alien Status

Clarified information obtained through the Federal Data Services HUB can only be used for Medicaid so we can't use any information found in ARIES to process other Public Assistance programs.

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Removed Alaska Literacy Program as a SNAP E&T provider. Alaska Literacy Program notified the Division they will not continue SNAP E&T services effective July 1, 2021

MS 602-1(I) Voluntary Job Quit and Reduction of Hours

Clarified if someone quits a job the same day they apply for UIB , UIB exemption may apply, but only if the individual applies for UIB prior to quitting the job.

MS 602-2 Resources

Updated the FFY 2022 SNAP resource limit standards released by FNS

MS 602-2(A) Definition and Types of Resources

Clarified SNAP application should not be denied solely on a household listing resources exceeding the resource limit within the SNAP application.

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Clarified Per Capita dividend payments derived from tribally managed gaming revenues are countable as income and a resource

MS 602-3(D) Exempt Income

Clarified to exempt income of an individual under age 18 and head of household, or employed and living with parents, verification of student status is required

MS 602-4 Deductions

Clarified regulations do not allow to pend for verification of expenses unless it affects the household's eligibility or allotment level

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Removed information about questionable shelter expenses. All shelter expenses must be verified

Updated the FFY 2022 homeless shelter deduction released by FNS

MS 604-2(E) Recertification

Removed "if questionable" as new or previously unreported and or unverified shelter costs are always verified

Removed INGENS as INGENS will no longer be accessible to eligibility workers

MS 604-3(C) Verification Requirements: Changes Reported During the Certification Period

Removed information indicating shelter costs are only needed when questionable. Verification of shelter costs is required

MS 604-3(E) Adding a Member to the Household

Clarified felony status must be verified before someone is added to the SNAP household

Addendum 4 SNAP Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2022 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2021-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2021

This year the Social Security Administration announced a Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 2022, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Clarified we are now able to accept telephonic applications and signatures for SNAP

MS 601-2(H) The Application Form

Clarified we are now able to accept telephonic applications and signatures for SNAP

MS 601-3(E) The Interview

Clarified we are now able to accept telephonic applications and signatures for SNAP

MS 602-2(B) Exempt Resources

Added Health Savings Account (HSA) as an exempt resource if trustee has set restrictions on the HSA so that funds may only be used for qualified medical expenses, and nothing else

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect the COLA update

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Clarified pet charges added to rent are not allowable shelter expense deductions

Clarified note section to remove the reference to the LIHEAP award list

Included the use of ECOS and when to contact Tribal agencies for LIHEAP verification

Added links to correlating HAP manual Addenda

MS 604-1 Certification Period

Changed verbiage to clarify after a household is determined eligible, most certification periods will be 6 months in length. Households in which all household members are elderly or disabled and have no earned income may be certified for up to 24 months.

MS 604-2 Recertification

Clarified we are now able to accept telephonic applications and signatures for SNAP

MS 604-3(F) Removing a Member from the Household

Clarified an individual who is restricted from leaving the home by way of ankle monitor is not considered incarcerated

MS 604-4(C) Notice Requirements During the Certification Period

Clarified an individual who is restricted from leaving the home by way of ankle monitor is not considered incarcerated

Addendum 6 Documentation of Eligible Alien Status

Clarified Afghan SIV s receiving the new "SQ4" and "SQ5 codes, are SIV s for the purposes of SNAP eligibility and should be treated in the same manner as those receiving codes "SQ" and "SI"

Addendum 9 State & FNS Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Added Chanlyut Treatment Program as a FNS approved treatment center

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health and Social Services

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2022-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2022

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines effective April 1, 2022. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4(A) Verification

Clarified the Prisoner Verification System (PVS) must be checked within IEVS at application and recertification.

Clarified caseworkers are required to check eDRS at application and recertification, as well as when an adult household member is added to the case

Clarified INME 13 ( SVES ) should be checked at application and recertification for death match.

MS 602-4(E) Deductions

Clarified for verification of heating costs when the person or agency the household is renting from charges for heat, the lease agreement or contact with the person or agency the household is renting from is an acceptable shelter cost verification.

MS 602-4(F) Deductions

Clarified a child support deduction is allowed only for children who are not living in the home.

MS 604-2(E) Recertification

Clarified the Prisoner Verification System (PVS) must be checked within IEVS at application and recertification.

Clarified caseworkers are required to check eDRS at application and recertification, as well as when an adult household member is added to the case

Clarified INME 13 ( SVES ) should be checked at application and recertification for death match.

MS 604-3(c) Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Added a bullet to clarify death match alerts received in EIS during the certification period should be worked.

MS 604-4(D) Notice Requirements During the Certification Period

Clarified notice is not required when benefits happen to increase due to a mass change.

MS 605-2(D) Specialized Budgeting

Clarified due to the nature of S-corporations in that income is passed through to the shareholder's pro-rata share, we need to obtain verification of the S-corporation tax forms verifying income that is said to have been received.

Updated the Self-Employment Standards due to FPL changes effective April 2022.

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2022-01 (04/22)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2022-02 (09/22)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2022-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 1, 2022

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2022. Added a new addendum similar to MAGI Addendum 3 showing examples of different incomes and whether they are considered countable income. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-3(E) Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Removed reference to Appointment Plus as this system is no longer used

MS 602-1(D) US Citizenship and Alien Status

Revised section to include Temporary Protected Status (TPS). TPS individuals are considered a legal, but non-qualified alien

MS 602-2 Resources

Updated the FFY 2023 SNAP resource limit standards released by FNS

MS 602-2(A) Definition and Types of Resources

Clarified the portion of virtual currency/cryptocurrency that can be readily converted to cash is considered a liquid resource

MS 602-2(B) Exempt Resources

Clarified if someone owns two properties not separated by intervening property owned by others, they are still considered two separate properties unless both properties have been joined legally in to one property

MS 602-4(E) Deductions

Updated the FFY 2023 homeless shelter deduction released by FNS

MS 602-4(F) Child Support Deduction

Clarified income verification from third parties such as on a pay stub or the UIB interface that show a child support garnishment, is not sufficient to show that child support is legally obligated.

MS 604-3 Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Clarified in situations where multiple changes are reported and we only have needed verification for some, but not all changes, we should work the changes where we have all needed verification, and take action to pend for the rest of the verification that is needed.

Addendum 4 SNAP Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2023 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

Addendum 9 Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Teen Challenge Pacific NW Adult and Teen Alaska CA added a new location that is considered a FNS approved substance abuse program. Added new location

Addendum 11 Countable and Excluded Income for SNAP

Added new addendum similar to MAGI Addendum 3 showing exmpales of different incomes and whether they are considered countable income

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2022-03 (12/22)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2022-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2022

This year the Social Security Administration announced a Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 2023, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

This year the department went through a name change. Department of Health and Social Services is now Department of Health. We changed references throughout the manual from Department of Health and Social Services to Department of Health, including any email addresses and websites.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

Throughought the manual

Changed all references from DHSS to DOH , including any email addresses and websites

MS 601-3(E) Scheduling Interviews

Removed mention of request for service in note section

Clarified the 2nd bullet in this section for further understanding

MS 601-4(A) Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Clarified INME 9 provides verification for Social Security

Clarified IEVS is used to check PFD and Quarterly Wage

Revised verbiage in charts for clarity

Removed "NFIN" from CSSD section as this is an acronym for the NSTAR , which is no longer used

MS 601-5(B) Benefit Start Date

Clarified we cannot change the benefit start date on applications where individuals were living in an ineligible institution, such as a drug and alcohol treatment center

MS 602-1(A)(5) Excluded Household Members

Clarified that the dates of the felony conviction questions, August 22, 1996 for drug felons and September 22, 1996 for IPV 's, in the GEN 50C are different

MS 602-1(D)(5) Non-Qualified Aliens

Added the list of documents for Temporary Protected Status (TPS) verification in a list format

MS 602-1(D)(6) Documentation of United States Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Updated link for USCIS forms

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change

MS 602-3(D)(18) Volunteer Payments

Clarified AmeriCorps VISTA payments are considered countable earned income, not unearned

MS 604-3(d) Reporting and Acting on Changes During the Certification Period

Clarified the policy regarding the EBT Transaction report and added a link for caseworkers to cross reference the updated EBT transaction process in Admin MS 112-4(B)

Added an example showing action is taken to adversely affect benefits when a GEN 61 is received from the funeral home indicating a SNAP recipient's passing

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2022-03 (12/22)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2023-01 (04/23)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2023-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 3, 2023

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the Federal Poverty Guidelines effective April 1, 2023. We also added a new addendum for the Social Security Enumeration Template. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-1(C) Nondiscrimination

Updated nondiscrimination statement to align with the current FNS nondiscrimination statement

MS 601-4(C) Questionable Information

Clarified client statement is acceptable verification for race, ethnicity, and gender.

MS 602-1(A)(5) Households

Added information regarding the new Felony Condition Verification Form (GEN 158)

MS 602-1(E) Social Security Numbers

Clarified there are some classes of aliens for whom the SSA will not normally issue an SSN , even though they are qualified aliens, unless they are required by law to have an SSN as a condition of receiving federally funded benefits. If this is the case for an applicant who is otherwise eligible for SNAP benefits, the caseworker must send a letter to the SSA explaining the need for an SSN for the purpose of qualifying for SNAP

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Partners for Progress is no longer contracted with the state as an E&T provider. Removed Partners for Progress as E&T provider

MS 602-2(B)(4) Exempt Resources

Clarified that certain 457 deferred compensation pension plans may be an excluded resource

MS 602-4(E)(6) Shelter Deduction

Updated instructions to code the homeless shelter deduction from OE AT to HS . This was due to a system fix needed for the homeless shelter deduction to calculate properly in EIS

MS 605-1(D) Institutions

Clarified that residents of FNS or Division of Behavioral Health approved institutions may receive SNAP while a resident, only if they are participating in a drug or alcohol related treatment program, offered by the institution

MS 605-2(D)(7) Annualization Determination

Updated the Self-Employment Annualization Standards to reflect updated FPL effective April 2023

Addendum 12 Social Security Enumeration Template

Created a new addendum for the Social Security Enumeration Template

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2023-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 1, 2023

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2023. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4(A) Verification Requirements for Initial Applications

Clarified IPV information is now checked in IEVS , not eDRS

MS 602-1(A)(5) Excluded Household Members

Removed mention of HowWeGuide. The Division no longer uses this guide

Clarified an individual is potentially ineligible for SNAP if they are convicted of a drug related felony that occurred on or before 2/7/14. We need to first look at the date the crime occurred.

MS 602-1(L) Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents

Updated ABAWD time limits based on the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023

Updated ABAWD exceptions based on the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023

Updated ABAWD 36-month period to match current 36-month period

Updated note that explains Alaska is ABAWD exempt through October 31, 2023

MS 602-2(B) (4) Exempt Resources

Clarified retirement accounts that are excluded from resources regardless of employment status

MS 602-4 Deductions

Updated the FFY 2024 homeless shelter deduction released by FNS

MS 604-1(A) Certification Period

Amended section "When Assigning a 24-month Certification Period" to align with Interim Report requirements at mid-certification

Added information regarding the division's change to authorize 12 and 24 month certification periods with the requirement to submit an interim report at the mid-point of the certification period

Removed bullet "to align with other programs" for circumstances where the caseworker can provide a shorter certification period. Statement does not align with federal SNAP certification length regulations

MS 604-2 Interim Report

Added information regarding the division's change to authorize 12 and 24 month certification periods with the requirement to submit an interim report at the mid-point of the certification period

MS 605-7(A)(2) Verification of PFD Income

Clarified note section to reflect we should be using IEVS and not INME 9 to verify PFD information

MS 606-3 Replacing SNAP benefits

Clarified SNAP benefits that are lost due to a household misfortune are limited to household misfortunes such as a fire, flood, loss of electricity, equipment malfunction, or other disaster

607-3 Claims

Added information regarding the requirement to establish claims before the last day of the quarter following the quarter in which the overpayment or trafficking incident was discovered

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2024 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2023-03 (12/23)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2023-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2023

This year the Social Security Administration announced a COLA increase effective January 2024, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

Highlights of this and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 Definitions

Clarified work definition regarding ABAWD

MS 601-3(A)(2) The Interview

Added link to MS 604-3(C) for clarity that households that may have the recertification interview waived are households in which all adult members are elderly and/or disabled and have no earned income.

MS 601-5(D) Denying the Application

Clarified that we cannot deny a SNAP application when applicants verbally indicate they are over the income or resource limit during the interview

MS 602-1(B) Residency

Clarified households that leave the state for purposes of residing in another state will have their cases closed for not meeting the residency requirement

MS 602-1(E) Social Security Numbers

Removed reference to Addendum 12 as it is being removed so it's not available to the public

MS 602-1(F)(3) ABAWD

Updated ABAWD age from 18 through 49 to 18 through 52

MS 602-1(G)(5) ABAWDs

Updated ABAWD exemptions to include discretionary exemptions, and homeless individuals, veterans, and individuals aging out of foster care as required by the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023.

Updated link to read SNAP MS 602-1G(2)

MS 602-1(L)(2) and (6) Able Bodied Adults Without Dependents

Revised note to align with the new ABAWD waiver time limit criteria beginning 11/01/2023 through 10/31/2024

Added discretionary exemption policy that will be used during the current ABAWD waiver

MS 602-3(B)(2) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change

602-4(E)(7) Shelter Costs

Clarified verification of shelter costs must be verified if the information is more than 60 days old, even changes that have not changed by more than $25, to align with federal regulations

604-1 Certification Period

Clarified households that are eligible for 24 month certification periods are households in which all adult household members are elderly or disabled and have no earned income

604-2(C) Interim Report: Household Fails To Act Or Requested Verifications Is Not Received

Revised to clarify the only time we close an individual with adequate notice is if the IR is not received

Clarified when households submit the IR untimely, but prior to the last day of the month in which it is due

604-3 Recertification

Clarified that a new review/recertification period cannot be established for SNAP /Medicaid when processing applications or recertifications for other programs

Fixed link from 604-2B to 604-3B. This was changed due to the new Interim Report section being added to the manual

604-3(C) Recertification: Caseworker Actions

Clarified households that may have the recertification interview waived are households in which all adult members are elderly and/or disabled and have no earned income, to align with the approved waiver language

604-3(E) Verification Requirements: Recertification

Clarified all income reported at recertification needs to be verified if the information is more than 60 days old, even changes that have not changed by more than $50, to align with federal regulations

605-7(A)(2) PFD Interface

Clarified caseworkers must check IEVS , not INME 9, when verifying PFD eligibility

Addendum 2

Revised to align with the new ABAWD waiver, effective 11/01/2023 - 10/31/2024

Addendum 11

Clarified foster care income is exempt, dependent on nature of foster care payments, and household living circumstances

Addendum 12

Removed Addendum 12 so the information is not available to the public

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2024-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2024

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the FPL effective April 1, 2024. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Clarified programs added to the original application can only be added if the interview is conducted timely (30 days, or 7 days for SNAP expedites)

MS 601-2(A) The Application Form

Added information regarding the SNAP Online Application

MS 601-4 Verification

Clarified we can only use income verification received from The Work Number after the client has reported income from the employer

MS 602-1(B) Residency

Clarified a SNAP recipient may be temporarily out of state. There is not a specified period before someone loses Alaska residency.

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Created and added a workflow chart for clarity on work requirement procedures

MS 602-2(B) Exempt Resources

Clarified monies withdrawn for purposes outside of allowable education expenses from 529 College Savings Plans are considered countable resources in the month withdrawn and may cause ineligibility if retained

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Updated the lottery and gambling winnings limit that was increased due to the 2024 SNAP COLA

MS 604-3 Recertification

Clarified the GEN 50C form can be used to recertify if submitted in the month or the month prior to the month the certification period ends

MS 604-4 Reporting and Acting on Changed During the Certification Period

Updated the lottery and gambling winnings limit that was increased due to the 2024 SNAP COLA

MS 604-4(E) Adding a Member to the Household

Clarified when to add newborn to a SNAP case during an existing certification period

MS 604-5(D) Adverse Actions Requiring No Notice

Clarified the requirement for advanced notice of adverse action in COLA mass change situations

MS 605-1(D) Institutions

Removed the link to the SharePoint site

MS 605-2(D)(7) Annualization Determination

Updated the Self-Employment Annualization Standards to reflect updated FPL effective April 2024

MS 605-3(B) Expedite Service

Clarified felony questions need to be answered prior to authorizing SNAP benefits

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2024-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 3, 2024

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2024. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Clarified an identifiable application can establish the application date, but the application cannot be processed to determine eligibility until a complete application has been received. Added a link to SNAP MS 601-2(H)

MS 601-2(H) Completing the Application Form

Clarified that completed applications must contain a signed and dated Acknowledgement of Understanding and Statement of Truth

MS 601-3(E) Scheduling Interviews

Added information regarding request for service applications

MS 602-1(B) Residency

Clarified the SNAP case is pended due to questionable residency if we cannot get hold of the client to verbally verify residency

MS 602-1(D) U.S. Citizenship and Eligible Alien Status

Clarified that COFA non-immigrants are no longer subject to the 5-year waiting period as long as they meet all other SNAP financial and non-financial eligibility requirements

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Added note regarding the ABAWD upper age limit requirements based on the Fiscal responsibility Act of 2023

MS 602-1(L) Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents

Updated ABAWD age requirements based on the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023

MS 602-2 Resources

Revised resource limit for SPECAT and non-SPECAT households due to the FFY 2025 SNAP COLA

MS 602-3(B) Unearned Income

Clarified the 25% SNAP deduction due to a penalty applies to work requirement penalties and fraud penalties

Updated the amount households are required to report for the receipt of substantial lottery/gambling winnings, from $4,250 to $4,500 due to the FFY 2025 SNAP COLA

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Updated the FFY 2025 homeless shelter deduction released by FNS

MS 604-2 Interim Report

Revised SNAP MS 604-2 to align with the timeliness specified in SNAP MS 604-2(B)(1)

MS 604-2(A) Interim Report Form

Clarified if a household submits a GEN 50C with SNAP marked at the time the time the FSP 95 is due, we may use the GEN 50C in place of the FSP 95 as long as the household provided all the information needed within the GEN 50C for the FSP 95

MS 604-2(B) Interim Report Filing Deadlines

Clarified if a household submits a GEN 72 with SNAP marked on the form in the month after case closure due to failing to submit the FSP 95 , we may use the GEN 72 as an initial application

MS 604-4 Reporting and Acting On Changes During the Certification Period

Updated the amount households are required to report for the receipt of substantial lottery/gambling winnings, from $4,250 to $4,500 due to the FFY 2025 SNAP COLA

MS 604-4(C) Verification Requirements: Changes Reported During the Certification Period

Updated income change reporting requirement from $100 to $125 caused by FFY COLA change

MS 605-1(D) Institutions

Clarified that listing of institutions in this section is not all inclusive

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2025 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS

Addendum 7 SNAP Budget Work Sheets

Updated FSP 74 and FSP 75 link to Eforms website

Revised outdated program name verbiage from Food Stamps to SNAP

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2024-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 2, 2024

This year the Social Security Administration announced a Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 2025, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

This update includes policy revisions to ABAWD exempt and non-exempt areas as FNS approved Alaska's waiver to exempt areas of Alaska from ABAWD work requirements due to high unemployment.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601 Application Process

Added applications submitted online to the acceptable application process

MS 601-2(A) What Is An Acceptable Application Form?

Revised section to align with verbiage used in other DPA manuals

MS 601-3(A) When Is An Interview Required?

Aligned recertification verbiage in 601-3(A) to match recertification procedure in 604-3

MS 601-4 Verification

Clarified that anytime previously verified information is more than 60 days old, it is considered outdated

MS 602-1(D)(6)(c) Proof of Qualified Alien Status

Added reference to the Administration for Children & Families' Shepherd HHS Letter Verification website for verifying interim assistance letters and eligibility letters for the victims of trafficking

MS 602-1(F) Work Requirements

Revised list of E&T providers

Revised ABAWD age limits 18 through 54 and removed note about the age increase that went into effect 10/01/24

MS 602-1(G)(5) Determining Work Requirements and Exemptions

Revised ABAWD age limit through the age of 54

Added link to 602-1(L)(6) after "Individuals that qualify for a discretionary exemption (policy determines this population)

MS 602-1(L) Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents

Removed "is age 18 through 52" and the effective date of the current ABAWD ages in first paragraph and under subsection 6, exemptions

Updated Discretionary Exemption population and effective dates to align with the new ABAWD waiver effective 11/01/24-10/31/25

MS 602-3(B)(2) Unearned Income

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change

MS 604-1 Certification Period

Added link to the newly created ESAP sub-section for SNAP households certified for 36 months

MS 604-2 Interim Report

Added link to the newly created ESAP sub-section for SNAP households certified for 36 months

MS 604-3 Recertification

Added link to the newly created ESAP sub-section for SNAP households certified for 36 months

MS 604-4(F) Removing a Member From the Household

Clarified when opening a new SNAP case for another household member when the PI moves out of the home, the household member must sign the Statement of Truth

MS 605-3(D) Elderly Simplified Application Project

Created new subsection to outline the Elderly Simplified Application Project (ESAP) demonstration policy

MS 605-7(G) PFD at Recertification

Revised X004 notice name to " SNAP Recertification"

Addendum 2 ABAWD Work Requirement Exempt and Non-Exempt Areas

Updated ABAWD exempt and non-exempt areas to reflect the revised ABAWD waiver, effective 11/01/2024-10/31/2025

Addendum 4 Alaska SNAP Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated to reflect Copper River Census Area should be in the South Central Utility Region

Addendum 9 State & FNS Approved Substance Abuse Programs

Removed Chanlyut and Cook Inlet Tribal Council as FNS approved treatment centers. They are no longer utilizing SNAP benefits for residents

Added Mount Juneau Counseling & Recovery as Division of Behavioral Health approved treatment center

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2025-01

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2025

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the FPL effective April 1, 2025. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

ENTIRE MANUAL

Revised all mention of "Food Stamps" or "FS" and replaced with "Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program/ SNAP "

Revised Child Support Services Division (CSSD) to Child Support Enforcement Division ( CSED )

MS 601 APPLICATION PROCESS

Added link to SNAP MS 605-3 C which clarifies that SNAP benefits cannot be delayed as a result of a household member applying for any other program

Added "authorized representative" to the list of submissions of an identifiable application

Added online application verbiage to the list of acceptable applications throughout this section

MS 601-2 D WHAT IS AN IDENTIFIABLE APPLICATION?

Revised verbiage to removed the word "form" in the first sentence of the paragraph

MS 601-4 A VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS FOR INITIAL APPLICATIONS

Clarified that eDRS is verified through IEVS on the interface verification table

Revised section for clarity

MS 602-1 B RESIDENCY

Clarified when caseworkers should consider Alaska residency questionable

MS 602-1 E SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBERS

Revised section to remove case processing information

MS 602-1 G (4) DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Added Kenai, Soldotna, and Wasilla to the locations where individuals may reside for voluntary participation in E&T

MS 602-1 L (2) ABAWDS

Updated 36-month period from January 2022 - December 2024 to January 2025 - December 2027

MS 602-2 B (7) EXEMPT RESOURCES

Clarified that monies held in a business bank account, which can be readily converted to cash, are considered a liquid resource as described in SNAP MS 602-2 A (1)

MS 602-4 DEDUCTIONS

Removed "Exemption" regarding an expense that results in a shelter deduction is only verified when questionable

MS 604-3 A RECERTIFICATION APPLICATION FORM

Revised manual section to align with division forms and removed information related to process

MS 604-3 B RECERTIFICATION: APPLICATION FILING DEADLINES

Clarified the ways a household may submit a recertification application

MS 604-3 E VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: RECERTIFICATION

Clarified that eDRS is verified through IEVS on the interface verification table

Added link to MS 601-4 A for information regarding data systems and EIS interfaces that must be checked at recertification

MS 604-4 A (3) ACTING ON REPORTED CHANGES

Added note to clarify what information must be verified before a reported change causes a SNAP case to close.

MS 605-2 D (7) INCOME FROM SELF EMPLOYMENT

Updated the Self-Employment Annualization Standards to reflect updated FPL effective April 2025

ADDENDUM 9 STATE & FNS APPROVED SUBSTANCE ABUSE PROGRAMS

Removed Alaska Dream Center as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed Drug & Alcohol Residential Treatment Program as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed Stepping Stones as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed KAR House as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed Ernie Turner Center as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed SouthCentral Foundation as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Removed Tanana Chiefs Conference, Inc. as an authorized SNAP treatment facility

Revised facility names for Teen Challenge Pacilic NW Adult and Teen Alaska CA to the correct facility names for the men and womens centers.
