body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2025-02

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: September 2, 2025

This update includes yearly changes to the income and utility standards that take effect October 1, 2025. We also added extensive revisions to Categorical Eligibility to include Broad Based Categorical Eligibility policy. Other changes are either technical in nature or clarifications that are the results of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 Definitions

Added definition for Broad Based Categorical Eligibility (BBCE).

Added definition for TANF Funded Non-Cash Benefit.

MS 601-5(D) Denying the Application

Added links to SNAP manual sections for verification requirements at initial application and recertification to determine proper denials of SNAP applications.

MS 602-1(A) Households

Clarified the notes in sub-sections (5)(g) and (5)(h) for the Drug Felony Qualifying Condition Verification form (GEN 157) and the Felony Qualifying Condition Verification Form (GEN 158) to align with the verification verbiage that these forms are also required for verifying a client is 'satisfactorily serving' a period of probation or parole.

MS 602-1(E) Social Security Numbers

Clarified further determining good cause for failure to comply with the requirement to apply for or provide a State agency with an SSN .

MS 602-4(E) Shelter Deduction

Clarified that a household is eligible for the SUA if the household lives in a multi-unit dwelling or an individual unit and receives a qualifying weatherization program payment per the 11/18/2024 FNS Final Rule.

Updated the FFY 2026 homeless shelter deduction released by FNS .

MS 604-2(A) Interim Report

Revised Note to include link to MS 601-2(A) for acceptable forms that can be used in lieu of the FSP 95 .

MS 605-6 Categorical Eligibility

Added Broad Based Categorical Eligibility (BBCE) policy.

MS 605-7(L) Permanent Fund Dividend Special Processing

Replaced link to Addendum 10 EIS Notices for PFD Hold Harmless with link to Admin MS 108-5 .

Addendum 1 City and Village Classification

Removed Geographic Area codes as they pertain to back-end system coding only.

Addendum 4 Program Standards and Maximum Allotments

Updated the FFY 2026 maximum allotments and utility standards released by FNS .

Added BBCE Gross Income Limits.

Addendum 7 SNAP Budget Work Sheets

Added entry and link to Eforms website for SNAP Budget Worksheet FSP 76 (06-4197) for Broad Based Categorical Eligibility.

Addendum 10 EIS Notices for PFD Hold Harmless

Removed Addendum 10 as PFD HH notices are listed in the Admin Manual.

Addendum 11 Countable and Excluded Income for SNAP

Renamed and relocated to Addendum 10 .

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2025-02 (09/25)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2025-03 (11/25)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2025-03

MANUAL: Alaska Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: November 1, 2025

On July 4, 2025, President Donald J. Trump signed the 2025 Reconciliation Bill (H.R. 1). The law contains several provisions that affect the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP). Changes related to provisions of H.R. 1 are effective November 1, 2025.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-3 A WHEN IS AN INTERVIEW REQUIRED?

Removed the note that is no longer applicable under the 12 or 24 month certification period: An interview is not required if a household submits their recertification after the certification period ends but within the following month, and they are not due for their annual interview.

MS 602-1 D US CITIZENSHIP AND ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Revised non-citizen eligibility policies, consistent with the provisions outlined in H.R. 1 .

MS 602-1 G DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Removed ABAWD policy requirements outlined in the general work requirements section and added link to the appropriate ABAWD section in SNAP MS 602-1(L)(6) .

MS 602-3 D EXEMPT INCOME

Removed note regarding potential heating utility standard deduction as this information is explained in SNAP MS 602-4

MS 602-4 DEDUCTIONS

Revised the exception to specify that expenses covered by LIHEAP or similar Enery Assistance payments are only allowable when the household includes an elderly or disabled member, in alignment with the provision outlined in H.R. 1 .

MS 602-4 E SHELTER DEDUCTIONS

Revised the section to specify that LIHEAP or similar Energy Assistance payments are only countable toward the HUD or excess shelter deduction when the household includes an elderly or disabled member, in alignment with the provisions outlined in H.R. 1 .

MS 605-7 PERMANENT FUND DIVIDEND SPECIAL PROCESSING

Updated the contact information in subsection (E)(2), changing the reference from DPA Policy to OCS .

Removed reference to INME 9 from 605-7(F) and replaced with IEVS .

ADDENDUM 2 ABAWD WORK REQUIREMENT EXEMPT AND NON-EXEMPT AREAS

Updated borough and census areas to align with Department of Labor.

ADDENDUM 6 DOCUMENTATION OF ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Revised non-citizen eligibility policies, consistent with the provisions outlined in the H.R. 1 .

ADDENDUM 10 COUNTABLE AND EXCLUDED INCOME FOR SNAP

Changed "Census Income" to "Census Income from 2020 Census"

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2025-03 (11/25)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2025-04 (12/25)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2025-04

MANUAL: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: December 1, 2025

Revised ABAWD policy to align with Alaska's Good Faith Exemption waiver, allowed under the provisions of the 2025 Reconciliation Bill (H.R. 1). This includes updated designations of exempt and non-exempt areas for the new waiver period: 11/01/25 - 10/31/26.

This update also includes policy revisions to Eligible Alien Status to align with the provisions from H.R. 1, further clarified in the Food and Nutrition Services memo dated 10/31/2025.

This year the Social Security Administration announces a Cost of Living Allowance (COLA) increase effective January 2026, which increased the payee fee that can be excluded as unearned income.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 600-2 DEFINITIONS

Added definitions for terms "Indian", "Urban Indian", and "California Indian".

MS 601-4 VERIFICATION

Replaced WINSTAR with ACSESS .

MS 602-1(D) US CITIZENSHIP AND ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Revised non-citizen eligibility policies, consistent with the provisions outlined in H.R. 1 and incorporating updated guidance from FNS .

MS 602-1(L) ABLE-BODIED ADULTS WITHOUT DEPENDENTS (ABAWDs)

Revised allowable ABAWD exemptions consistent with Alaska's Good Faith Waiver exemptions from the provisions outlined in H.R. 1.

MS 602-3(B) UNEARNED INCOME

Updated payee fees to reflect COLA change.

MS 605-6 CATEGORICAL ELIGIBILITY

Revised subsection to specify only CE households have certain factors of eligibility verified by another program, and that BBCE households must have ALL factors of eligibility verified.

Addendum 2 ABAWD WORK REQUIREMENT EXEMPT AND NON-EXEMPT AREAS

Updated ABAWD exempt and non-exempt areas to reflect the revised ABAWD waiver, effective 11/01/2025 - 10/31/2026.

Addendum 6 DOCUMENTATION OF ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Removed and reserved this section for future use. All information previously located in this Addendum can be found in MS 602-1(D) .

Addendum 8 LIST OF COMMUNITIES BY CENSUS AREAS

Revised borough and census areas based on changes made by the Department of Labor.

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2025-04 (12/25)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2026-01 (04/26)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2026-01

MANUAL: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: April 1, 2026

We revised the self-employment annualization table based on changes in the FPL effective April 1, 2026. We also added a dedicated manual section for Rural Interview Postponement. Other changes are either technical in nature, due to clarifications provided by FNS, or as a result of policy questions or suggestions from the field.

Highlights of these and other policy revisions are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-2(H) THE APPLICATION FORM

Updated this section to include online applications as an option for individuals to apply.

MS 601-3(A) THE INTERVIEW

Clarified that interviews must be completed at least once every 12 months.

MS 602-1(D) CITIZENSHIP AND ELIGIBLE ALIEN STATUS

Updated Qualified Aliens Eligible with No Waiting Period to align with FNS guidance.

MS 602-1(F) WORK REQUIREMENTS

Revised list of E&T providers.

MS 602-1(G) DETERMINING WORK REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

Expanded areas served by the self-directed E&T program to include the Municipality of Anchorage, Mat-Su Borough, and Central Kenai Peninsula.

MS 602-2(E) TRANSFER OF RESOURCES

Updated example to reflect the current resource limit of $3,000.

MS 602-4(C) DEPENDENT CARE DEDUCTION

Clarified that a dependent care deduction expense must be verified if the previously verified information is outdated.

MS 604-4 REPORTING AND ACTING ON CHANGES DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Added National Directory of New Hires (NDNH) match as a change that must be worked when reported during the certification period.

MS 604-4(C) VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: CHANGES REPORTED DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Updated verification threshold for income changes reported during the certification period to changes of $50.00 or more.

Updated shelter cost change verification policy to align with SNAP MS 602-4(E)(7) .

Added note detailing when information is considered outdated.

MS 604-5(D) NOTICE REQUIREMENTS DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Updated Note to clarify when notices are required in Mass Change situations.

MS 605-2(D) INCOME FROM SELF EMPLOYMENT

Updated the Self-Employment Annualization Standards to reflect updated FPL effective April 2026.

Removed language that indicates SNAP households with self employment earned income above the annualization threshold are not eligible for SNAP.

MS 605-3(B) EXPEDITED SERVICE

Revised to clarify that verification will be completed within the expedited service timeframe whenever possible. If any verification is postponed to meet the required application processing timeframe, the information and amounts claimed by the household will be used.

MS 605-4(C) RURAL INTERVIEW POSTPONEMENT

Added new subsection regarding Rural Interview Postponement.

MS 608-2 ADMINISTRATIVE DISQUALIFICATION HEARINGS

Removed reference to the Gen 40 Fraud Complaint form and replaced with a link to Admin Manual Section 112-4(A) for fraud referral processes.

metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2026-01 (04/26)

metadata.source_authority: Alaska Department of Health Division of Public Assistance
metadata.title: Alaska SNAP Manual: 2026-02 (07/26)

body:
STATE OF ALASKA

Department of Health

Division of Public Assistance

TRANSMITTAL NUMBER: 2026-02

MANUAL: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program

DATE: July 1, 2026

We have updated the minimum pend time frame given to households to provide requested information from 10 days to 15 days.

Highlights of this policy revision are described below. If you have any questions, please contact the Policy & Program Development team at hss.dpa.policy@alaska.gov .

OVERVIEW OF CHANGES

MS 601-4 VERIFICATION

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.

MS 601-5 ACTIONS TAKEN ON THE APPLICATION

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.

MS 604-2 INTERIM REPORT

Revised example to use a 15 day pend.

MS 604-3 RECERTIFICATION

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.

Revised example to use a 15 day pend.

MS 604-4(A) ACTING ON REPORTED CHANGES

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.

MS 604-4(C) VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: CHANGES REPORTED DURING THE CERTIFICATION PERIOD

Revised example to use a 15 day pend.

MS 604-4(E) ADDING A MEMBER TO THE HOUSEHOLD

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.

MS 605-3(B) EXPEDITED SERVICE

Replaced 10 day pend with 15 day pend for requested information.
